en.tldr: This paper studies a class of generalized mean-reflected McKean-Vlasov type backward stochastic differential equations, combining a mean reflection constraint on expectation with a generalized integral with respect to a continuous non-decreasing process, and proves existence and uniqueness of solutions.
en.motivation: To extend the theoretical framework of McKean-Vlasov type BSDEs by combining mean reflection constraints with generalized integral structures.
en.method: Uniqueness proved via stability estimates; existence proved using a penalization method combined with smooth approximation of the obstacle.
en.result: Established existence and uniqueness theorems for solutions to this class of equations.
en.help_to_user: Provides rigorous existence and uniqueness theory for McKean-Vlasov systems with mean reflection constraints and generalized integral structures.

idea_spark.idea: Combining mean reflection constraints with more general integral processes could be extended to systems driven by jump processes or fractional Brownian motion.
idea_spark.risk: The specific form of generalized integral may limit applicability; verification for practical models is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: This framework could potentially be applied to financial pricing or mean-field game problems with expectation constraints.

papers.title: TokenGS: Decoupling 3D Gaussian Prediction from Pixels with Learnable Tokens
papers.summary: In this work, we revisit several key design choices of modern Transformer-based approaches for feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) prediction. We argue that the common practice of regressing Gaussian means as depths along camera rays is suboptimal, and instead propose to directly regress 3D mean coordinates using only a self-supervised rendering loss. This formulation allows us to move from the standard encoder-only design to an encoder-decoder architecture with learnable Gaussian tokens, thereby unbinding the number of predicted primitives from input image resolution and number of views. Our resulting method, TokenGS, demonstrates improved robustness to pose noise and multiview inconsistencies, while naturally supporting efficient test-time optimization in token space without degrading learned priors. TokenGS achieves state-of-the-art feed-forward reconstruction performance on both static and dynamic scenes, producing more regularized geometry and more balanced 3DGS distribution, while seamlessly recovering emergent scene attributes such as static-dynamic decomposition and scene flow.

ai.tldr: TokenGS 是一种基于 Transformer 的前馈式 3D 高斯溅射预测方法，它通过可学习的高斯令牌解耦了 3D 高斯预测与像素的绑定，提升了重建的鲁棒性和几何正则化。
ai.motivation: 现有基于 Transformer 的前馈式 3DGS 预测方法通常沿相机射线回归高斯均值作为深度，这种方法被认为不够理想。
ai.method: 提出直接回归 3D 均值坐标，仅使用自监督渲染损失。采用编码器-解码器架构，引入可学习的高斯令牌，使预测基元数量与输入图像分辨率和视图数量解耦。
ai.result: 在静态和动态场景上实现了最先进的前馈重建性能，产生了更正则化的几何形状和更平衡的 3DGS 分布，并能自然地恢复静态-动态分解和场景流等场景属性。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要从多视图图像进行快速、鲁棒 3D 重建的用户提供了一种新方法，该方法对位姿噪声和多视图不一致性具有更好的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将 3D 场景表示为一组可学习的、与输入分辨率无关的令牌，通过解码器生成具体的 3D 属性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于 Transformer 架构和大规模训练数据，计算成本可能较高；自监督渲染损失的优化可能具有挑战性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将“预测”任务重新定义为“从一组可学习的原型中进行解码”，从而获得更好的泛化性和灵活性。

en.tldr: TokenGS is a Transformer-based feed-forward method for 3D Gaussian Splatting prediction that decouples 3D Gaussian prediction from pixels via learnable Gaussian tokens, improving reconstruction robustness and geometric regularization.
en.motivation: Current Transformer-based feed-forward 3DGS prediction methods commonly regress Gaussian means as depths along camera rays, which is argued to be suboptimal.
en.method: Proposes to directly regress 3D mean coordinates using only a self-supervised rendering loss. Employs an encoder-decoder architecture with learnable Gaussian tokens, unbinding the number of predicted primitives from input image resolution and number of views.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art feed-forward reconstruction performance on both static and dynamic scenes, producing more regularized geometry and more balanced 3DGS distribution, while seamlessly recovering emergent scene attributes such as static-dynamic decomposition and scene flow.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new method for users requiring fast and robust 3D reconstruction from multi-view images, with improved robustness to pose noise and multiview inconsistencies.

idea_spark.idea: Represent a 3D scene as a set of learnable tokens independent of input resolution, and decode them into specific 3D attributes.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on Transformer architecture and large-scale training data, potentially incurring high computational cost; optimization of the self-supervised rendering loss may be challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires rethinking 'prediction' tasks as 'decoding from a set of learnable prototypes' for better generalization and flexibility.

papers.title: TRACER: Trace-Based Adaptive Cost-Efficient Routing for LLM Classification
papers.summary: Every call to an LLM classification endpoint produces a labeled input-output pair already retained in production logs. These pairs constitute a free, growing training set: a lightweight surrogate trained on them can absorb a significant portion of future traffic at near-zero marginal inference cost. The open questions are when the surrogate is reliable enough to deploy, what it handles versus defers, and how that boundary evolves as data accumulates.   We introduce TRACER (Trace-based Adaptive Cost-Efficient Routing), an open-source system that trains ML surrogates on an LLM's own production traces and governs deployment through a parity gate: the surrogate is activated only when its agreement with the LLM exceeds a user-specified threshold α. To make the routing boundary transparent, TRACER generates interpretability artifacts describing which input regions the surrogate handles, where it plateaus, and why it defers.   On a 77-class intent benchmark with a Sonnet 4.6 teacher, TRACER achieves 83-100% surrogate coverage depending on the quality target α; on a 150-class benchmark, the surrogate fully replaces the teacher. On a natural language inference task, the parity gate correctly refuses deployment because the embedding representation cannot support reliable separation. The system is available as open-source software.

ai.tldr: TRACER 是一个基于 LLM 生产日志训练轻量级代理模型，并通过一致性门控实现自适应、低成本路由的开源系统。
ai.motivation: 每次调用 LLM 分类端点都会产生已保留的输入-输出对，这些数据构成了免费、不断增长的训练集。训练一个轻量级代理模型可以吸收大量未来流量，且推理成本几乎为零。关键问题在于何时部署代理模型、它处理什么内容、以及边界如何随数据积累而演变。
ai.method: TRACER 在 LLM 自身生产轨迹上训练机器学习代理模型，并通过一致性门控管理部署：仅当代理模型与 LLM 的一致性超过用户指定阈值 α 时才激活。系统生成可解释性工件，以透明展示代理模型处理的输入区域、性能瓶颈及推迟原因。
ai.result: 在 77 类意图基准测试中，TRACER 根据质量目标 α 实现了 83-100% 的代理覆盖率；在 150 类基准测试中，代理模型完全替代了教师模型。在自然语言推理任务中，一致性门控正确拒绝了部署，因为嵌入表示无法支持可靠分离。
ai.help_to_user: 该系统提供了一种利用现有生产数据自动构建低成本替代方案的方法，帮助用户在保证质量的前提下显著降低 LLM 调用成本。

idea_spark.idea: 利用生产中的输入-输出轨迹作为免费训练数据，构建轻量级替代模型。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型可能无法处理分布外或复杂样本，过度依赖可能导致质量下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将生产系统中的“副产品”数据转化为优化资源效率的资产。

en.tldr: TRACER is an open-source system that trains lightweight surrogate models on LLM production logs and implements adaptive, low-cost routing through a parity gate.
en.motivation: Every call to an LLM classification endpoint produces labeled input-output pairs retained in production logs, constituting a free, growing training set. A lightweight surrogate trained on them can absorb significant future traffic at near-zero marginal inference cost. The open questions are when to deploy the surrogate, what it handles versus defers, and how that boundary evolves with accumulating data.
en.method: TRACER trains ML surrogates on an LLM's own production traces and governs deployment through a parity gate: the surrogate is activated only when its agreement with the LLM exceeds a user-specified threshold α. On a natural language inference task, the parity gate correctly refuses deployment because the embedding representation cannot support reliable separation.
en.help_to_user: The system provides a method to automatically build low-cost alternatives using existing production data, helping users significantly reduce LLM invocation costs while maintaining quality guarantees.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize input-output traces from production as free training data to construct lightweight replacement models.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate models may fail on out-of-distribution or complex samples; over-reliance could lead to quality degradation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about how to transform 'byproduct' data from production systems into assets for optimizing resource efficiency.

papers.title: Time-Dependent Logarithmic Perturbation Theory for Quantum Dynamics: Formulation and Applications
papers.summary: We present a time-dependent extension of logarithmic perturbation theory for nonrelativistic quantum dynamics governed by the Schrödinger equation, in which the logarithm of the wave function is expanded in powers of a coupling constant. The resulting hierarchy of equations defining the perturbative corrections is governed by a gauge-rotated Hamiltonian of the unperturbed system and leads to closed-integral expressions for the time-dependent corrections based on Duhamel's formula. This closed-integral structure of corrections is a hallmark of time-independent logarithmic perturbation theory and is preserved in the present extension. This structure, in particular, provides a computable expression for the instantaneous energy shift. Furthermore, dynamic energy shifts arise naturally within this framework in the form of time-averaged expectation values of pseudopotentials and can be related, for example, to AC Stark shifts and electric polarizabilities. As an illustration, we apply the method to the harmonic oscillator and the hydrogen atom, both driven by a time-dependent laser field. The harmonic oscillator provides a proof of principle for which the exact solution is recovered, while the hydrogen atom illustrates the method applied to atomic systems. Supported by numerical simulations, we demonstrate the applicability to obtain relevant physical observables with high accuracy. The present approach offers a promising alternative for analytical studies of time-dependent multi-photon processes in the perturbative regime.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种时间依赖的对数微扰理论，用于求解含时薛定谔方程，通过波函数对数的耦合常数展开，得到基于Duhamel公式的封闭积分修正表达式，并应用于谐振子和氢原子系统。

en.tldr: This work presents a time-dependent extension of logarithmic perturbation theory for quantum dynamics governed by the Schrödinger equation, expanding the logarithm of the wave function in powers of a coupling constant, yielding closed-integral corrections via Duhamel's formula, with applications to driven harmonic oscillators and hydrogen atoms.
en.motivation: There is a need for an analytical framework that preserves the closed-integral structure of time-independent logarithmic perturbation theory while handling time-dependent quantum systems, particularly for computing instantaneous and dynamic energy shifts like AC Stark shifts.
en.method: Expand the logarithm of the wave function in powers of a coupling constant, derive a hierarchy of equations governed by a gauge-rotated unperturbed Hamiltonian, and obtain time-dependent corrections as closed integrals using Duhamel's formula.
en.result: The method recovers the exact solution for the harmonic oscillator and is successfully applied to the hydrogen atom, with numerical simulations demonstrating high accuracy in obtaining physical observables such as instantaneous energy shifts and electric polarizabilities.
en.help_to_user: Offers an alternative analytical tool for studying time-dependent multi-photon processes in the perturbative regime, especially useful for computing energy shifts and polarizabilities in laser-driven atomic systems.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the closed-integral hallmark of time-independent logarithmic perturbation theory to time-dependent systems via gauge rotation
idea_spark.risk: Limited to perturbative regimes; may not apply to strong-coupling or non-perturbative processes
idea_spark.inspiration: The framework could be generalized to other time-driven quantum systems or combined with numerical methods for enhanced computational efficiency

papers.title: Solitonic Solutions of the One-Dimensional Harmonically Trapped Repulsive Bose-Einstein Condensate via Neural Network Quantum States
papers.summary: We demonstrate the existence of bright solitons in a repulsively interacting, harmonically trapped quasi-one-dimensional Bose-Einstein condensate described by the Gross-Pitaevskii equation. Using a neural-network quantum state (NNQS) approach, we parametrize the initial wavefunction and optimize it to find solutions that recur after one trap period, effectively balancing repulsion with trap-induced attraction. Aside from the bright solitonic solution, we also report double bright and dark soliton states. Perturbing the initial state with multiplicative phase and amplitude noise confirms that these periodic orbits are orbitally stable. Our results indicate that NNQS provides a powerful framework for uncovering coherent structures in nonlinear wave systems.

ai.tldr: 使用神经网络量子态方法，在谐振势阱中排斥相互作用的准一维玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体中发现了亮孤子解，并证明了其轨道稳定性。
ai.motivation: 探索非线性波系统中相干结构的数值发现方法，特别是排斥相互作用下玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚体的孤子解。
ai.method: 采用神经网络量子态参数化初始波函数，通过优化寻找在一个陷阱周期后重现的解，平衡排斥作用与陷阱诱导的吸引。
ai.result: 发现了亮孤子、双亮孤子和暗孤子态，并通过相位和振幅噪声扰动证实了这些周期轨道的轨道稳定性。
ai.help_to_user: 为使用神经网络方法研究非线性量子系统的相干结构提供了具体案例和验证。

idea_spark.idea: 将NNQS框架扩展到更高维或更复杂相互作用势的非线性波系统。
idea_spark.risk: 神经网络优化的收敛性和解的唯一性需要严格验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 机器学习方法可能系统化发现非线性物理模型中未知的解析解或稳定结构。

en.tldr: Using a neural-network quantum state approach, bright soliton solutions are found in a harmonically trapped repulsive quasi-1D Bose-Einstein condensate, with orbital stability demonstrated.
en.motivation: To explore numerical discovery methods for coherent structures in nonlinear wave systems, particularly soliton solutions in repulsively interacting Bose-Einstein condensates.
en.method: Parametrizing the initial wavefunction with neural-network quantum states and optimizing to find solutions recurring after one trap period, balancing repulsion with trap-induced attraction.
en.result: Bright soliton, double bright soliton, and dark soliton states are reported, with multiplicative phase and amplitude noise confirming orbital stability of these periodic orbits.
en.help_to_user: Provides a concrete case study and validation for using neural network methods to study coherent structures in nonlinear quantum systems.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the NNQS framework to nonlinear wave systems with higher dimensions or more complex interaction potentials.
idea_spark.risk: Convergence of neural network optimization and uniqueness of solutions require rigorous verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Machine learning methods may systematize the discovery of unknown analytical solutions or stable structures in nonlinear physical models.

papers.title: Global existence for a system without self-diffusion and different mobilities
papers.summary: We study a one-dimensional cross-diffusion system for two interacting populations on the torus, with a linear pressure law and different mobilities. For arbitrary bounded non-negative initial data, we show that any good approximation scheme, yields existence of global weak solutions. More precisely, we introduce a notion of \textit{admissible approximation sequence} and show that any such sequence admits a subsequence converging to a weak solution of the system. The strategy relies on entropy estimates and the div--curl lemma, in the framework of Young measures.

ai.tldr: 研究一维环面上两种相互作用种群、具有线性压力律和不同迁移率的交叉扩散系统。对于任意有界非负初值，证明了任何良好逼近格式都能得到全局弱解的存在性。
ai.motivation: 交叉扩散系统在种群动力学等应用中常见，但缺乏自扩散项和具有不同迁移率时，其全局解的存在性理论尚不完善。
ai.method: 引入“容许逼近序列”的概念，利用熵估计、div-curl引理和Young测度框架，证明任何此类序列都存在收敛于系统弱解的子序列。
ai.result: 对于任意有界非负初值，系统存在全局弱解。
ai.help_to_user: 为无自扩散、具有不同迁移率的交叉扩散系统提供了系统的全局存在性理论框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将解的存在性问题转化为对逼近序列的收敛性分析，通过熵控制获得紧性。
idea_spark.risk: 方法高度依赖于系统的特定结构（一维、线性压力律、环面），推广到更一般情形可能需要额外假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可尝试应用于其他缺乏标准抛物正则性的扩散系统或高阶相互作用模型。

en.tldr: Studies a one-dimensional cross-diffusion system for two interacting populations on the torus with linear pressure law and different mobilities. For arbitrary bounded non-negative initial data, proves that any good approximation scheme yields existence of global weak solutions.
en.motivation: Cross-diffusion systems arise in applications like population dynamics, but global existence theory for systems without self-diffusion and with different mobilities is not fully established.
en.method: Introduces the notion of an 'admissible approximation sequence', and uses entropy estimates, the div-curl lemma, and the Young measures framework to show any such sequence has a subsequence converging to a weak solution.
en.result: Global weak solutions exist for arbitrary bounded non-negative initial data.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous global existence theory framework for cross-diffusion systems without self-diffusion and with different mobilities.

idea_spark.idea: Reformulate the existence problem into convergence analysis of approximation sequences, using entropy to obtain compactness.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies heavily on the specific structure (1D, linear pressure law, torus); generalization to more general settings may require additional assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: This framework could be attempted for other diffusion systems lacking standard parabolic regularity or for models with higher-order interactions.

papers.title: A counter-example to persistence in generalised preferential attachment trees
papers.summary: Consider a generalised preferential attachment tree with attachment function $f$, that is a random tree, where at each time-step a node connects to an existing node $v$ with probability proportional to $f(\mathrm{deg}(v))$, where $\mathrm{deg}(v)$ denotes the degree of the node in the existing tree. We provide a counter-example to a conjecture of the author asserting that under the assumption $\sum_{j=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{f(j)^2} < \infty$ there is a persistent hub in the model, that is, a single node that has the maximal degree for all but finitely many time-steps. The counter-example is a minor modification of a related counter-example due to Galganov and Ilienko.

ai.tldr: 本文提供了一个反例，反驳了广义优先附着树中存在持久枢纽的猜想。
ai.motivation: 作者先前猜想：当附件函数满足特定条件时，广义优先附着树中会存在一个持久枢纽节点（即除有限时间步外始终拥有最大度）。本文旨在检验该猜想的正确性。
ai.method: 对Galganov和Ilienko提出的相关反例进行微小修改，构造出一个新的反例模型。
ai.result: 成功构造出反例，证明原猜想不成立，即即使满足∑1/f(j)²<∞条件，持久枢纽也可能不存在。
ai.help_to_user: 澄清了广义优先附着树中持久枢纽存在的条件，为相关随机图模型的理论研究提供了重要修正。

idea_spark.idea: 通过微调已知反例的参数或结构来生成新反例。
idea_spark.risk: 反例可能高度依赖特定参数选择，难以推广。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对随机图中枢纽节点持久性条件的更精细分析。

en.tldr: This paper provides a counter-example to the conjecture that a persistent hub exists in generalised preferential attachment trees.
en.motivation: The author previously conjectured that under a certain condition on the attachment function, a persistent hub (a single node with maximal degree for all but finitely many time-steps) would exist. This work aims to test the validity of that conjecture.
en.method: A minor modification of a related counter-example due to Galganov and Ilienko is constructed.
en.result: A counter-example is successfully constructed, disproving the original conjecture—showing that even when ∑1/f(j)²<∞, a persistent hub may not exist.
en.help_to_user: Clarifies the conditions for persistent hub existence in generalised preferential attachment trees, providing an important correction to the theory of such random graph models.

idea_spark.idea: Generate new counter-examples by subtly adjusting parameters or structure of known related counter-examples.
idea_spark.risk: The counter-example might rely heavily on specific parameter choices, making generalization difficult.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires more refined analysis of conditions for hub persistence in random graphs.

papers.title: HyperSpace: A Generalized Framework for Spatial Encoding in Hyperdimensional Representations
papers.summary: Vector Symbolic Architectures (VSAs) provide a well-defined algebraic framework for compositional representations in hyperdimensional spaces. We introduce HyperSpace, an open-source framework that decomposes VSA systems into modular operators for encoding, binding, bundling, similarity, cleanup, and regression. Using HyperSpace, we analyze and benchmark two representative VSA backends: Holographic Reduced Representations (HRR) and Fourier Holographic Reduced Representations (FHRR). Although FHRR provides lower theoretical complexity for individual operations, HyperSpaces modularity reveals that similarity and cleanup dominate runtime in spatial domains. As a result, HRR and FHRR exhibit comparable end-to-end performance. Differences in memory footprint introduce additional deployment trade-offs where HRR requires approximately half the memory of FHRR vectors. By enabling modular, system-level evaluation, HyperSpace reveals practical trade-offs in VSA pipelines that are not apparent from theoretical or operator-level comparisons alone.

ai.tldr: HyperSpace是一个开源框架，将向量符号架构系统分解为模块化操作符，用于编码、绑定、捆绑、相似性、清理和回归。通过分析HRR和FHRR两种后端，发现相似性和清理操作在空间域中主导运行时，导致两者端到端性能相当，但HRR内存占用约为FHRR的一半。
ai.motivation: 向量符号架构为超维空间中的组合表示提供了定义良好的代数框架，但缺乏系统级的模块化评估工具来揭示实际部署中的权衡。
ai.method: 开发HyperSpace框架，将VSA系统分解为编码、绑定、捆绑、相似性、清理和回归等模块化操作符，并使用该框架对HRR和FHRR两种代表性VSA后端进行分析和基准测试。
ai.result: 尽管FHRR单个操作的理论复杂度较低，但模块化分析显示相似性和清理操作在空间域中主导运行时，使得HRR和FHRR端到端性能相当。HRR的内存占用约为FHRR向量的一半，引入了额外的部署权衡。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了模块化的VSA评估框架，帮助研究人员系统分析不同VSA后端的实际性能特征和部署权衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂表示系统分解为标准化操作模块，通过组合测试揭示隐藏的性能瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 框架的通用性可能掩盖特定应用场景的特殊优化需求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对计算复杂度和内存占用之间权衡的系统化研究，特别是在资源受限环境中。

en.tldr: HyperSpace is an open-source framework that decomposes Vector Symbolic Architecture systems into modular operators for encoding, binding, bundling, similarity, cleanup, and regression. Analysis of HRR and FHRR backends reveals that similarity and cleanup dominate runtime in spatial domains, resulting in comparable end-to-end performance, with HRR requiring approximately half the memory of FHRR vectors.
en.motivation: Vector Symbolic Architectures provide a well-defined algebraic framework for compositional representations in hyperdimensional spaces, but lack modular system-level evaluation tools to reveal practical deployment trade-offs.
en.method: Develop the HyperSpace framework to decompose VSA systems into modular operators for encoding, binding, bundling, similarity, cleanup, and regression, using it to analyze and benchmark two representative VSA backends: HRR and FHRR.
en.result: Although FHRR provides lower theoretical complexity for individual operations, modular analysis reveals that similarity and cleanup dominate runtime in spatial domains, making HRR and FHRR exhibit comparable end-to-end performance. HRR requires approximately half the memory of FHRR vectors, introducing additional deployment trade-offs.
en.help_to_user: Provides a modular VSA evaluation framework to help researchers systematically analyze the practical performance characteristics and deployment trade-offs of different VSA backends.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex representation systems into standardized operational modules to reveal hidden performance bottlenecks through combinatorial testing.
idea_spark.risk: The framework's generality may obscure special optimization requirements for specific application scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires systematic research on trade-offs between computational complexity and memory footprint, especially in resource-constrained environments.

papers.title: Efficient Search of Implantable Adaptive Cells for Medical Image Segmentation
papers.summary: Purpose: Adaptive skip modules can improve medical image segmentation, but searching for them is computationally costly. Implantable Adaptive Cells (IACs) are compact NAS modules inserted into U-Net skip connections, reducing the search space compared with full-network NAS. However, the original IAC framework still requires a 200-epoch differentiable search for each backbone and dataset. Methods: We analyzed the temporal behavior of operations and edges within IAC cells during differentiable search on public medical image segmentation benchmarks. We found that operations selected in the final discrete cell typically emerge among the strongest candidates early in training, and their architecture parameters stabilize well before the final epoch. Based on this, we propose a Jensen--Shannon-divergence-based stability criterion that tracks per-edge operation-importance distributions and progressively prunes low-importance operations during search. The accelerated framework is called IAC-LTH. Results: Across four public benchmarks (ACDC, BraTS, KiTS, AMOS), several 2-D U-Net backbones, and a 2-D nnU-Net pipeline, IAC-LTH discovers IAC cells whose patient-level segmentation performance matches and sometimes slightly exceeds that of cells found by the original full-length search, while reducing wall-clock NAS cost by 3.7x to 16x across datasets and backbones. These results are consistent across architectures, benchmarks, and both non-augmented and augmented training settings, while preserving the gains of IAC-equipped U-Nets over strong attention-based and dense-skip baselines. Conclusion: Competitive IAC architectures can be identified from early-stabilizing operations without running the full search, making adaptive skip-module design more practical for medical image segmentation under realistic computational constraints.

ai.tldr: 提出IAC-LTH框架，通过基于Jensen-Shannon散度的稳定性准则在搜索过程中逐步剪枝低重要性操作，将植入式自适应细胞（IAC）的搜索成本降低3.7-16倍，同时保持分割性能。
ai.motivation: 植入式自适应细胞（IAC）能提升医学图像分割性能，但其可微分搜索过程需要200个epoch，计算成本高昂。

en.tldr: Proposes IAC-LTH framework that reduces search cost for Implantable Adaptive Cells (IACs) by 3.7-16× while maintaining segmentation performance, using a Jensen-Shannon-divergence-based stability criterion to progressively prune low-importance operations during search.
en.motivation: Implantable Adaptive Cells (IACs) improve medical image segmentation but require computationally expensive 200-epoch differentiable searches for each backbone and dataset.
en.method: Analyzes temporal behavior of operations/edges during IAC differentiable search, finding that selected operations stabilize early. Proposes Jensen-Shannon-divergence-based stability criterion to track per-edge operation-importance distributions and progressively prune low-importance operations.

idea_spark.idea: Monitor convergence of architecture parameter distributions to early-terminate or prune searches in neural architecture search.
idea_spark.risk: Premature pruning may eliminate operations that become important later; stability thresholds require careful tuning.

papers.title: Sidorenko-Inspired Pessimistic Estimation
papers.summary: Recently, Abo Khamis et al. showed how to upper bound the size of a join of multiple tables, a problem essential to query optimization in database theory. They unified earlier works by the following information-theoretical framework.   1. Let $(X_1,..., X_n)$ be a row selected from the join uniformly at random.   2. The size of the join is now $\exp(H(X_1,..., X_n))$.   3. To upper bound $H(X_1,..., X_n)$, break it into several $\textit{local entropies}$, such as $H(X_1)$, $H(X_2, X_3)$, and $H(X_4|X_5)$, using Shannon-type inequalities.   4. Upper bound local entropies using statistics of the tables being joined.   The statistics Abo Khamis et al. considered are the counts of graph homomorphisms from stars to the tables. In a follow-up work, we generalized stars to bi-stars. In this paper, we generalize bi-stars to caterpillars, an even larger class of graphs inspired by Sidorenko's conjecture. Simulations show that, while Abo Khamis et al.'s star bound overestimates the join size by $m$, our bi-star bound overestimates by about $m^{3/4}$, and this paper's new caterpillar bound overestimates by about $m^{3/5}$. These exponents are obtained by log-log regressions with R-square $> 0.98$. All homomorphisms are counted in time linear in the size of the tables being joined.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于毛毛虫图的连接大小悲观估计方法，改进了先前基于星图和双星图的估计，将过估计指数从m降至约m^(3/5)。
ai.motivation: 数据库查询优化需要准确估计多表连接结果的大小。Abo Khamis等人提出的基于星图的估计方法存在较大过估计，后续的双星图方法有所改进，但仍需进一步提升精度。
ai.method: 1. 将连接大小表示为随机选择行的联合熵的指数。2. 使用香农型不等式将联合熵分解为局部熵。3. 利用从毛毛虫图到表的图同态计数来上界局部熵。4. 毛毛虫图是受Sidorenko猜想启发的比双星图更大的图类。
ai.result: 模拟实验显示：星图边界过估计约为m，双星图边界过估计约为m^(3/4)，而新的毛毛虫图边界过估计约为m^(3/5)。所有指数均通过R平方>0.98的对数回归获得。
ai.help_to_user: 为数据库查询优化提供了更精确的连接大小上界估计方法，可帮助优化器生成更高效的查询计划。

idea_spark.idea: 使用更复杂的图结构（如毛毛虫图）来捕获表中更丰富的统计信息，从而改进熵的上界估计。
idea_spark.risk: 计算毛毛虫图的同态计数可能比星图和双星图更复杂，但文中提到可在表大小的线性时间内完成。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将组合数学和图论中的其他猜想或结构应用于信息论和数据库优化问题。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a pessimistic join size estimation method based on caterpillar graphs, improving upon previous star and bi-star bounds by reducing the overestimation exponent from m to approximately m^(3/5).
en.motivation: Query optimization in databases requires accurate estimation of the size of multi-table joins. The star-based bound by Abo Khamis et al. suffers from significant overestimation, and the subsequent bi-star bound offers improvement but still requires further precision enhancement.
en.method: 1. Express join size as the exponent of the joint entropy of a randomly selected row. 2. Decompose the joint entropy into local entropies using Shannon-type inequalities. 3. Upper bound local entropies using counts of graph homomorphisms from caterpillar graphs to the tables. 4. Caterpillar graphs are a larger class of graphs inspired by Sidorenko's conjecture, generalizing bi-stars.
en.result: Simulations show: the star bound overestimates by about m, the bi-star bound by about m^(3/4), and the new caterpillar bound by about m^(3/5). All exponents are obtained via log-log regressions with R-square > 0.98.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more accurate upper bound for join size estimation in database query optimization, aiding the optimizer in generating more efficient query plans.

idea_spark.idea: Using more complex graph structures (like caterpillars) to capture richer statistical information from tables, thereby improving entropy upper bounds.
idea_spark.risk: Counting homomorphisms for caterpillar graphs might be more complex than for stars and bi-stars, but the paper states it can be done in linear time in the size of the tables.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the application of other conjectures or structures from combinatorics and graph theory to information theory and database optimization problems.

papers.title: Anderson Acceleration for Linearly Converging SQP-Type Methods
papers.summary: Although Anderson acceleration (AA) is known to speed up fixed-point iterations, it is rarely applied in constrained optimization, in particular sequential quadratic programming (SQP). We show that the local convergence behavior of a general family of (inexact) SQP-type methods can benefit from AA and introduce a simple heuristic to alleviate slower convergence farther from the solution. The method is implemented in the software framework acados. Numerical examples from optimal control illustrate consistent improvements in convergence of different SQP-type methods.

ai.tldr: 本研究将Anderson加速技术应用于SQP类方法，提升了约束优化的局部收敛速度，并在acados软件中实现，通过最优控制数值算例验证了效果。
ai.motivation: Anderson加速已知能加速定点迭代，但在约束优化特别是序列二次规划中应用很少。
ai.method: 将Anderson加速应用于一族（不精确）SQP型方法，并引入启发式策略缓解远离解时的慢收敛问题，在acados框架中实现。
ai.result: 最优控制数值算例表明，该方法能一致改善不同SQP型方法的收敛性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为约束优化问题提供了加速收敛的实用算法改进，可直接集成于现有SQP求解器。

idea_spark.idea: 在SQP每次迭代后应用Anderson加速修正步长，并设计启发式规则动态调整加速权重。
idea_spark.risk: 远离最优解时加速效果可能不稳定，需额外启发式策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 经典数值加速技术与现代优化算法的结合，可尝试扩展到其他迭代类优化方法。

idea_spark.inspiration: 经典数值加速技术与现代优化算法的结合，可尝试扩展到其他迭代类优化方法。

en.tldr: This work applies Anderson acceleration to SQP-type methods, improving local convergence rates for constrained optimization, implements it in the acados software, and validates performance with optimal control numerical examples.
en.motivation: Although Anderson acceleration is known to speed up fixed-point iterations, it is rarely applied in constrained optimization, particularly sequential quadratic programming.
en.method: Apply Anderson acceleration to a family of (inexact) SQP-type methods, introduce a heuristic to alleviate slower convergence farther from the solution, and implement it in the acados framework.
en.result: Numerical examples from optimal control illustrate consistent improvements in convergence of different SQP-type methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical algorithmic improvement for accelerating convergence in constrained optimization, directly integrable into existing SQP solvers.

idea_spark.idea: Apply Anderson acceleration to correct step sizes after each SQP iteration, with heuristic rules to dynamically adjust acceleration weights.
idea_spark.risk: Acceleration may be unstable far from the optimum, requiring additional heuristic strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining classical numerical acceleration techniques with modern optimization algorithms could be extended to other iterative optimization methods.

papers.title: Tight Sample Complexity Bounds for Best-Arm Identification Under Bounded Systematic Bias
papers.summary: As search depth increases in autonomous reasoning and embodied planning, the candidate action space expands exponentially, heavily taxing computational budgets. While heuristic pruning is a common countermeasure, it operates without formal safety guarantees when surrogate models (like LLMs) exhibit systematic evaluation biases. This paper frames the node expansion process as a localized Best-Arm Identification (BAI) problem over dynamic frontiers, subject to a bounded systematic bias $L$. By inverting the Lambert W function, we establish an additive sample complexity of $\mathcal{O}((Δ-4L)^{-2})$, which indicates that safe node elimination is only feasible when the empirical reward gap exceeds $4L$. We complement this with an information-theoretic lower bound of $Ω((Δ-2L)^{-2})$ to confirm the structural limits of biased search. Subsequent evaluations on both synthetic trees and complex reasoning tasks demonstrate that adhering to this local safety boundary successfully preserves optimal trajectories while maximizing sample allocation efficiency.

ai.tldr: 本文在存在有界系统偏差L的情况下，将节点扩展过程建模为动态边界上的局部最佳臂识别问题，推导出样本复杂度上界O((Δ-4L)^{-2})，并证明仅当经验奖励差距超过4L时才能安全剪枝。
ai.motivation: 自主推理和具身规划中，搜索深度增加导致候选动作空间指数级扩张，计算负担沉重。启发式剪枝是常用对策，但当替代模型（如LLM）存在系统评估偏差时，缺乏形式化安全保证。
ai.method: 将节点扩展过程建模为受有界系统偏差L约束的动态边界局部最佳臂识别问题，通过反演Lambert W函数推导样本复杂度上界，并建立信息论下界。
ai.result: 理论分析表明安全节点消除仅当经验奖励差距Δ > 4L时可行，样本复杂度上界为O((Δ-4L)^{-2})，下界为Ω((Δ-2L)^{-2})。在合成树和复杂推理任务上的评估验证了该局部安全边界能保持最优轨迹并最大化样本分配效率。
ai.help_to_user: 为存在系统偏差的搜索和规划问题提供了形式化的安全剪枝准则与样本复杂度界限。

idea_spark.idea: 利用偏差边界L与奖励差距Δ的关系，建立可证明的安全剪枝条件。
idea_spark.risk: 实际中系统偏差L可能难以精确估计或随时间变化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可将类似框架扩展到多智能体协作搜索或存在对抗性偏差的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用偏差边界L与奖励差距Δ的关系，建立可证明的安全剪枝条件。

idea_spark.inspiration: 可将类似框架扩展到多智能体协作搜索或存在对抗性偏差的场景。

en.tldr: This paper frames node expansion as a localized Best-Arm Identification problem under bounded systematic bias L, deriving a sample complexity upper bound O((Δ-4L)^{-2}) and proving safe pruning is only feasible when the empirical reward gap exceeds 4L.
en.motivation: As search depth increases in autonomous reasoning and embodied planning, the candidate action space expands exponentially. Heuristic pruning lacks formal safety guarantees when surrogate models exhibit systematic evaluation biases.
en.method: Model node expansion as a localized BAI problem over dynamic frontiers subject to bias L, invert the Lambert W function to derive sample complexity bounds, and establish information-theoretic lower bounds.
en.result: Theoretical analysis shows safe node elimination requires Δ > 4L, with upper bound O((Δ-4L)^{-2}) and lower bound Ω((Δ-2L)^{-2}). Evaluations on synthetic trees and reasoning tasks confirm this local safety boundary preserves optimal trajectories while maximizing sample efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides formal safety pruning criteria and sample complexity bounds for search and planning problems with systematic bias.

idea_spark.idea: Establish provably safe pruning conditions using the relationship between bias bound L and reward gap Δ.
idea_spark.risk: Systematic bias L may be difficult to estimate precisely or may vary over time in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could extend similar frameworks to multi-agent collaborative search or scenarios with adversarial bias.

papers.title: FAIR Universe Weak Lensing ML Uncertainty Challenge: Handling Uncertainties and Distribution Shifts for Precision Cosmology
papers.summary: Weak gravitational lensing, the correlated distortion of background galaxy shapes by foreground structures, is a powerful probe of the matter distribution in our universe and allows accurate constraints on the cosmological model. In recent years, high-order statistics and machine learning (ML) techniques have been applied to weak lensing data to extract the nonlinear information beyond traditional two-point analysis. However, these methods typically rely on cosmological simulations, which poses several challenges: simulations are computationally expensive, limiting most realistic setups to a low training data regime; inaccurate modeling of systematics in the simulations create distribution shifts that can bias cosmological parameter constraints; and varying simulation setups across studies make method comparison difficult. To address these difficulties, we present the first weak lensing benchmark dataset with several realistic systematics and launch the FAIR Universe Weak Lensing Machine Learning Uncertainty Challenge. The challenge focuses on measuring the fundamental properties of the universe from weak lensing data with limited training set and potential distribution shifts, while providing a standardized benchmark for rigorous comparison across methods. Organized in two phases, the challenge will bring together the physics and ML communities to advance the methodologies for handling systematic uncertainties, data efficiency, and distribution shifts in weak lensing analysis with ML, ultimately facilitating the deployment of ML approaches into upcoming weak lensing survey analysis.

ai.tldr: FAIR Universe弱引力透镜机器学习不确定性挑战赛发布首个包含多种真实系统误差的基准数据集，旨在解决训练数据有限和分布偏移问题，推动机器学习在宇宙学中的应用。
ai.motivation: 弱引力透镜是探测宇宙物质分布的有力工具，但传统高阶统计和机器学习方法依赖昂贵的宇宙学模拟，面临数据量少、系统误差建模不准导致的分布偏移、以及不同研究间方法难以比较的挑战。
ai.method: 构建首个包含多种真实系统误差的弱引力透镜基准数据集，并组织两阶段挑战赛，聚焦在有限训练数据和潜在分布偏移下从弱透镜数据中测量宇宙基本性质，为不同方法提供标准化比较基准。
ai.result: 该挑战赛将物理和机器学习社区聚集在一起，旨在推进处理系统不确定性、数据效率和分布偏移的方法论，最终促进机器学习方法在即将开展的弱引力透镜巡天分析中的部署。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个标准化的弱引力透镜基准数据集和挑战框架，帮助研究者评估和比较在数据有限和存在分布偏移情况下机器学习方法的鲁棒性与准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 为特定科学领域（如宇宙学）创建包含可控系统误差的基准数据集，并围绕其组织社区挑战，以系统性地评估和推进方法在真实挑战（如数据有限、分布偏移）下的表现。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟中引入的系统误差可能无法完全覆盖真实观测中的所有复杂性；挑战赛的成功高度依赖社区的参与度和所提交方法的多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将机器学习中的不确定性量化、分布鲁棒性等通用问题，通过领域特定的基准和挑战形式具体化，能有效驱动跨学科方法创新和社区协作。

en.tldr: The FAIR Universe Weak Lensing ML Uncertainty Challenge releases the first benchmark dataset with realistic systematics, aiming to address limited training data and distribution shifts to facilitate ML deployment in cosmology.
en.motivation: Weak lensing is a powerful probe of cosmic matter distribution, but high-order statistics and ML methods rely on expensive cosmological simulations, facing challenges of scarce training data, distribution shifts from inaccurate systematic modeling, and difficult cross-study method comparison.
en.method: Construct the first weak lensing benchmark dataset with several realistic systematics and launch a two-phase challenge focused on measuring fundamental universe properties from weak lensing data under limited training and potential distribution shifts, providing a standardized benchmark for rigorous method comparison.
en.result: The challenge brings together the physics and ML communities to advance methodologies for handling systematic uncertainties, data efficiency, and distribution shifts, ultimately facilitating ML deployment in upcoming weak lensing surveys.
en.help_to_user: Provides a standardized weak lensing benchmark dataset and challenge framework to help researchers evaluate and compare the robustness and accuracy of ML methods under data scarcity and distribution shifts.

idea_spark.idea: Create a benchmark dataset with controlled systematics for a specific scientific domain (e.g., cosmology) and organize a community challenge around it to systematically evaluate and advance methods against realistic challenges like data scarcity and distribution shifts.
idea_spark.risk: Systematics introduced in simulations may not fully capture all complexities in real observations; challenge success heavily depends on community participation and diversity of submitted methods.
idea_spark.inspiration: Concretizing general ML problems like uncertainty quantification and distributional robustness through domain-specific benchmarks and challenges can effectively drive interdisciplinary method innovation and community collaboration.

papers.title: Renormalised two-point functions of CLE$_4$ gaskets
papers.summary: We consider nested CLE$_4$ in a simply-connected domain and compute the following renormalised probabilities: the probability that two points belong to the same CLE$_4$ gasket and the probability that two points belong to the outermost CLE$_4$ gasket. While the integrability is rooted in the conformal field theory of the Ashkin-Teller (AT) model, we provide a purely probabilistic calculation via Brownian loop soups and the geometry of the 2D continuum Gaussian free field. More generally, we also calculate renormalised probabilities that two points belong to CLE$_4$ gaskets sampled in alternation with certain two-valued sets of the Gaussian free field. These quantities correspond to the two-point function of the conjectured scaling limit of the AT single spins on the critical line. At the decoupling point, our results recover the Ising model correlations and suggest a CLE$_4$-based FK representation of the AT spin model.

ai.tldr: 本文计算了嵌套CLE$_4$中两点属于同一或最外层gasket的归一化概率，连接了布朗环汤、高斯自由场与Ashkin-Teller模型的共形场论。
ai.motivation: 研究CLE$_4$ gasket的几何与概率性质，以理解Ashkin-Teller模型单自旋标度极限的两点函数。
ai.method: 使用布朗环汤和二维连续高斯自由场的几何进行纯概率计算，并计算交替采样CLE$_4$ gasket与高斯自由场二值集的归一化概率。
ai.result: 得到了归一化概率的显式表达式，在解耦点恢复了Ising模型关联，并提出了AT自旋模型的基于CLE$_4$的FK表示。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了连接共形场论、概率几何与统计物理模型的桥梁，展示了CLE$_4$在理解临界现象中的核心作用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用布朗环汤和高斯自由场的几何结构直接计算CLE$_4$ gasket的关联概率。
idea_spark.risk: 方法高度依赖于二维共形不变性和特定模型的精确可积性，推广到其他维度或模型可能困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将其他可积模型的关联函数解释为随机几何对象的概率，建立场论与随机几何的新对应。

en.tldr: This paper computes renormalised probabilities for two points belonging to the same or outermost CLE$_4$ gasket in nested CLE$_4$, linking Brownian loop soups, Gaussian free field, and conformal field theory of the Ashkin-Teller model.
en.motivation: To study geometric and probabilistic properties of CLE$_4$ gaskets for understanding the two-point function of the conjectured scaling limit of Ashkin-Teller single spins.
en.method: Purely probabilistic calculation via Brownian loop soups and geometry of the 2D continuum Gaussian free field, also computing renormalised probabilities for alternating sampling of CLE$_4$ gaskets with two-valued sets.
en.result: Explicit expressions for renormalised probabilities are obtained, recovering Ising correlations at the decoupling point and suggesting a CLE$_4$-based FK representation of the AT spin model.
en.help_to_user: Provides a bridge between conformal field theory, probabilistic geometry, and statistical physics models, demonstrating the central role of CLE$_4$ in understanding critical phenomena.

idea_spark.idea: Using geometric structures of Brownian loop soups and Gaussian free field to directly compute correlation probabilities of CLE$_4$ gaskets.
idea_spark.risk: The approach heavily relies on 2D conformal invariance and exact integrability of specific models, making generalization to other dimensions or models challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires interpreting correlation functions of other integrable models as probabilities of random geometric objects, establishing new correspondences between field theory and random geometry.

papers.title: Bridging scalp and intracranial EEG in BCI via pretrained neural representations and geometric constraint embedding
papers.summary: Electroencephalography (EEG) has become one of the key modalities underpinning brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) due to its high temporal resolution, rapid responsiveness, non-invasiveness, low cost, and portability. However, EEG signals are substantially inferior to intracranial EEG (iEEG) in signal-to-noise ratio and local spatial resolution, whereas iEEG suffers from extremely limited clinical accessibility owing to its invasive nature, hindering widespread application. To address this challenge, this study proposes a unified data-and prior knowledge-driven framework for EEG-iEEG representational enhancement. Guided by the principle that "geometric structure dictates function", the framework maps static cortical anatomy onto dynamic constraints governing neural signal propagation and integrates general-purpose neural representations extracted by a pre-trained large EEG model to explicitly model signal transmission through the brain. Enhanced EEG signals are then synthesized via a multidimensional representation diffusion process. Numerous experimental results demonstrate that the generated enhanced EEG signals effectively recover the neural activity patterns lost during propagation through the brain. This finding indicates that the performance ceiling of BCIs is constrained not only by acquisition hardware but also by the depth to which the generative model resolves the mechanisms of neural signal propagation. Collectively, the proposed framework provides a viable pathway toward acquiring high-fidelity neural signals at low cost.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出一个统一的数据与先验知识驱动框架，通过预训练的神经表征和几何约束嵌入，将头皮EEG信号增强至接近颅内EEG的质量，以低成本获取高保真神经信号。
ai.motivation: 头皮EEG信号信噪比和空间分辨率远低于颅内EEG，但颅内EEG因侵入性而临床可及性极低，限制了BCI的广泛应用。
ai.method: 基于'几何结构决定功能'原则，将静态皮层解剖映射为神经信号传播的动态约束，整合预训练大EEG模型提取的通用神经表征，通过多维表征扩散过程合成增强EEG信号。
ai.result: 生成的增强EEG信号有效恢复了大脑传播过程中丢失的神经活动模式，表明BCI性能上限不仅受采集硬件限制，也受生成模型解析神经信号传播机制的深度制约。
ai.help_to_user: 为低成本获取高保真神经信号提供了可行路径，可能提升基于EEG的脑机接口性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用生成模型解析神经信号传播机制，而非仅依赖硬件改进，来突破BCI性能瓶颈。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于预训练EEG模型的质量和几何约束的准确性，在个体解剖变异下可能泛化受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从'信号传播机制建模'角度而非单纯'信号处理'角度提升非侵入式神经信号质量。

en.tldr: This study proposes a unified data-and prior knowledge-driven framework that enhances scalp EEG signals to near-iEEG quality via pretrained neural representations and geometric constraint embedding, enabling low-cost acquisition of high-fidelity neural signals.
en.motivation: Scalp EEG signals are substantially inferior to iEEG in signal-to-noise ratio and spatial resolution, while iEEG suffers from extremely limited clinical accessibility due to its invasiveness, hindering widespread BCI application.
en.method: Guided by the principle that 'geometric structure dictates function', the framework maps static cortical anatomy onto dynamic constraints governing neural signal propagation, integrates general-purpose neural representations from a pre-trained large EEG model, and synthesizes enhanced EEG signals via a multidimensional representation diffusion process.
en.result: The generated enhanced EEG signals effectively recover neural activity patterns lost during brain propagation, indicating that BCI performance ceiling is constrained not only by acquisition hardware but also by the depth to which the generative model resolves neural signal propagation mechanisms.
en.help_to_user: Provides a viable pathway toward acquiring high-fidelity neural signals at low cost, potentially improving performance of EEG-based brain-computer interfaces.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage generative models to resolve neural signal propagation mechanisms, rather than relying solely on hardware improvements, to break through BCI performance bottlenecks.
idea_spark.risk: Depends on quality of pretrained EEG models and accuracy of geometric constraints; may generalize poorly under individual anatomical variations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires improving non-invasive neural signal quality from the perspective of 'modeling signal propagation mechanisms' rather than merely 'signal processing'.

papers.title: KVNN: Learnable Multi-Kernel Volterra Neural Networks
papers.summary: Higher-order learning is fundamentally rooted in exploiting compositional features. It clearly hinges on enriching the representation by more elaborate interactions of the data which, in turn, tends to increase the model complexity of conventional large-scale deep learning models. In this paper, a kernelized Volterra Neural Network (kVNN) is proposed. The key to the achieved efficiency lies in using a learnable multi-kernel representation, where different interaction orders are modeled by distinct polynomial-kernel components with compact, learnable centers, yielding an order-adaptive parameterization. Features are learned by the composition of layers, each of which consists of parallel branches of different polynomial orders, enabling kVNN filters to directly replace standard convolutional kernels within existing architectures. The theoretical results are substantiated by experiments on two representative tasks: video action recognition and image denoising. The results demonstrate favorable performance-efficiency trade-offs: kVNN consistently yields reduced model (parameters) and computational (GFLOPs) complexity with competitive and often improved performance. These results are maintained even when trained from scratch without large-scale pretraining. In summary, we substantiate that structured kernelized higher-order layers offer a practical path to balancing expressivity and computational cost in modern deep networks.

ai.tldr: 提出KVNN，一种可学习的多核Volterra神经网络，通过可学习的多核表示对不同阶交互进行建模，在保持性能的同时降低模型复杂度和计算成本。
ai.motivation: 高阶学习依赖于数据更精细的交互来丰富表示，但会增加传统大规模深度学习模型的复杂度。需要一种方法在保持表达力的同时控制计算成本。
ai.method: 使用可学习的多核表示，不同交互阶由具有紧凑可学习中心的不同多项式核组件建模，实现阶自适应参数化。特征通过层组合学习，每层包含不同多项式阶的并行分支。
ai.result: 在视频动作识别和图像去噪任务上验证，KVNN在保持竞争力的性能下，显著减少了模型参数和计算复杂度（GFLOPs），且无需大规模预训练。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可直接替换标准卷积核的高阶滤波器，为平衡深度网络表达力与计算成本提供了实用路径。

idea_spark.idea: 将Volterra级数与可学习核结合，构建结构化核化高阶层
idea_spark.risk: 多项式核组件的设计和优化可能增加训练复杂性
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将高阶交互建模与核方法结合，用于其他需要丰富特征表示的任务

en.tldr: Proposes KVNN, a learnable multi-kernel Volterra neural network that models different interaction orders via learnable multi-kernel representation, reducing model and computational complexity while maintaining performance.
en.motivation: Higher-order learning relies on more elaborate data interactions to enrich representations but increases complexity in conventional deep learning models. A method is needed to balance expressivity with computational cost.
en.method: Uses a learnable multi-kernel representation where distinct polynomial-kernel components with compact, learnable centers model different interaction orders, enabling order-adaptive parameterization. Features are learned through composition of layers with parallel branches of different polynomial orders.
en.result: Validated on video action recognition and image denoising tasks, KVNN achieves competitive performance while significantly reducing model parameters and computational complexity (GFLOPs), even without large-scale pretraining.
en.help_to_user: Provides higher-order filters that can directly replace standard convolutional kernels, offering a practical approach to balance expressivity and computational cost in deep networks.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Volterra series with learnable kernels to construct structured kernelized higher-order layers
idea_spark.risk: Design and optimization of polynomial-kernel components may increase training complexity
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining higher-order interaction modeling with kernel methods for other tasks requiring rich feature representations

papers.title: Geometrically Consistent Multi-View Scene Generation from Freehand Sketches
papers.summary: We tackle a new problem: generating geometrically consistent multi-view scenes from a single freehand sketch. Freehand sketches are the most geometrically impoverished input one could offer a multi-view generator. They convey scene intent through abstract strokes while introducing spatial distortions that actively conflict with any consistent 3D interpretation. No prior method attempts this; existing multi-view approaches require photographs or text, while sketch-to-3D methods need multiple views or costly per-scene optimisation.   We address three compounding challenges; absent training data, the need for geometric reasoning from distorted 2D input, and cross-view consistency, through three mutually reinforcing contributions: (i) a curated dataset of $\sim$9k sketch-to-multiview samples, constructed via an automated generation and filtering pipeline; (ii) Parallel Camera-Aware Attention Adapters (CA3) that inject geometric inductive biases into the video transformer; and (iii) a Sparse Correspondence Supervision Loss (CSL) derived from Structure-from-Motion reconstructions.   Our framework synthesizes all views in a single denoising process without requiring reference images, iterative refinement, or per-scene optimization. Our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art two-stage baselines, improving realism (FID) by over 60% and geometric consistency (Corr-Acc) by 23%, while providing up to a 3.7$\times$ inference speedup.

ai.tldr: 提出首个从单张手绘草图生成几何一致多视角场景的方法，通过新数据集、几何注意力适配器和对应监督损失解决草图空间扭曲与一致性难题。
ai.motivation: 手绘草图是几何信息最贫乏的多视角生成输入，现有方法需要照片、文本或多视角输入，无法从单张抽象扭曲的草图直接生成一致3D场景。
ai.method: 构建约9k草图-多视角数据集；设计并行相机感知注意力适配器（CA3）为视频Transformer注入几何偏置；提出基于运动恢复结构的稀疏对应监督损失（CSL）。
ai.result: 单次去噪过程合成所有视角，无需参考图像或逐场景优化。在真实感（FID）上提升超60%，几何一致性（Corr-Acc）提升23%，推理速度加快3.7倍。
ai.help_to_user: 为从抽象手绘草图快速生成3D一致场景提供端到端解决方案，降低3D内容创作门槛。

idea_spark.idea: 通过自动化流程构建草图-多视角配对数据集，解决训练数据缺失问题。
idea_spark.risk: 草图抽象程度和扭曲可能影响复杂场景生成质量，几何一致性监督依赖运动恢复结构的准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将多视角生成视为视频合成问题，用视频Transformer统一处理时空一致性，为跨模态3D生成提供新思路。

zh.tldr: 提出首个从单张手绘草图生成几何一致多视角场景的方法，通过新数据集、几何注意力适配器和对应监督损失解决草图空间扭曲与一致性难题。
zh.motivation: 手绘草图是几何信息最贫乏的多视角生成输入，现有方法需要照片、文本或多视角输入，无法从单张抽象扭曲的草图直接生成一致3D场景。
zh.method: 构建约9k草图-多视角数据集；设计并行相机感知注意力适配器（CA3）为视频Transformer注入几何偏置；提出基于运动恢复结构的稀疏对应监督损失（CSL）。
zh.result: 单次去噪过程合成所有视角，无需参考图像或逐场景优化。在真实感（FID）上提升超60%，几何一致性（Corr-Acc）提升23%，推理速度加快3.7倍。
zh.help_to_user: 为从抽象手绘草图快速生成3D一致场景提供端到端解决方案，降低3D内容创作门槛。

en.tldr: Proposes the first method to generate geometrically consistent multi-view scenes from a single freehand sketch, addressing sketch distortion and consistency challenges via a new dataset, geometric attention adapters, and correspondence supervision.
en.motivation: Freehand sketches are the most geometrically impoverished input for multi-view generation; existing methods require photographs, text, or multiple views, failing to generate consistent 3D scenes directly from abstract, distorted sketches.
en.method: Constructs ~9k sketch-to-multiview dataset via automated pipeline; designs Parallel Camera-Aware Attention Adapters (CA3) to inject geometric biases into video transformer; proposes Sparse Correspondence Supervision Loss (CSL) derived from Structure-from-Motion.
en.result: Synthesizes all views in a single denoising process without reference images or per-scene optimization. Improves realism (FID) by over 60% and geometric consistency (Corr-Acc) by 23%, with up to 3.7× inference speedup.
en.help_to_user: Provides an end-to-end solution for generating 3D-consistent scenes from abstract freehand sketches, lowering the barrier for 3D content creation.

idea_spark.idea: Constructs sketch-to-multiview paired dataset via automated pipeline to address missing training data.
idea_spark.risk: Sketch abstraction and distortion may affect complex scene generation quality; geometric consistency supervision relies on accuracy of Structure-from-Motion reconstructions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treats multi-view generation as video synthesis, using video transformer to unify spatiotemporal consistency, offering new perspective for cross-modal 3D generation.

papers.title: DVFace: Spatio-Temporal Dual-Prior Diffusion for Video Face Restoration
papers.summary: Video face restoration aims to enhance degraded face videos into high-quality results with realistic facial details, stable identity, and temporal coherence. Recent diffusion-based methods have brought strong generative priors to restoration and enabled more realistic detail synthesis. However, existing approaches for face videos still rely heavily on generic diffusion priors and multi-step sampling, which limit both facial adaptation and inference efficiency. These limitations motivate the use of one-step diffusion for video face restoration, yet achieving faithful facial recovery alongside temporally stable outputs remains challenging. In this paper, we propose, DVFace, a one-step diffusion framework for real-world video face restoration. Specifically, we introduce a spatio-temporal dual-codebook design to extract complementary spatial and temporal facial priors from degraded videos. We further propose an asymmetric spatio-temporal fusion module to inject these priors into the diffusion backbone according to their distinct roles. Evaluation on various benchmarks shows that DVFace delivers superior restoration quality, temporal consistency, and identity preservation compared to recent methods. Code: https://github.com/zhengchen1999/DVFace.

ai.tldr: DVFace是一个用于真实世界视频人脸修复的一步扩散框架，通过时空双码本提取互补先验，实现高质量、时序一致的身份保持修复。
ai.motivation: 现有基于扩散的视频人脸修复方法严重依赖通用扩散先验和多步采样，限制了面部适应性和推理效率。一步扩散在实现忠实面部恢复和时序稳定输出方面仍具挑战。
ai.method: 提出时空双码本设计，从退化视频中提取互补的空间和时序面部先验；设计非对称时空融合模块，根据先验的不同角色将其注入扩散主干网络。
ai.result: 在多个基准测试中，DVFace在修复质量、时序一致性和身份保持方面优于近期方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为真实世界退化视频的人脸修复提供了一个高效、高质量的一步扩散解决方案，显著提升了时序稳定性和身份保真度。

idea_spark.idea: 将面部先验解耦为空间（细节、身份）和时序（运动、连贯性）组件，并设计非对称注入策略。
idea_spark.risk: 对严重退化或极端姿态的视频，先验提取可能不充分；一步采样可能限制生成多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将任务特定先验结构化并适配到高效生成框架中，平衡质量与速度。

en.tldr: DVFace is a one-step diffusion framework for real-world video face restoration, using a spatio-temporal dual-codebook to extract complementary priors, achieving high-quality, temporally coherent, and identity-preserving restoration.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion-based video face restoration methods heavily rely on generic diffusion priors and multi-step sampling, limiting facial adaptation and inference efficiency. Achieving faithful facial recovery with temporal stability via one-step diffusion remains challenging.
en.method: Introduces a spatio-temporal dual-codebook design to extract complementary spatial and temporal facial priors from degraded videos; proposes an asymmetric spatio-temporal fusion module to inject these priors into the diffusion backbone according to their distinct roles.
en.result: Evaluation on various benchmarks shows DVFace delivers superior restoration quality, temporal consistency, and identity preservation compared to recent methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, high-quality one-step diffusion solution for real-world degraded video face restoration, significantly improving temporal stability and identity fidelity.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple facial priors into spatial (detail, identity) and temporal (motion, coherence) components, and design an asymmetric injection strategy.
idea_spark.risk: Prior extraction may be insufficient for severely degraded or extreme-pose videos; one-step sampling may limit generation diversity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires structuring task-specific priors and adapting them into efficient generative frameworks to balance quality and speed.

papers.title: $L^p$-Hodge decomposition and global integral estimates on the Cartan group
papers.summary: The study of Sobolev and Poincaré inequalities for differential forms in Carnot groups and in the more general sub-Riemannian setting is still an open problem in its full generality. One may conjecture that, for general Carnot groups, these inequalities are expressed in terms of suitable graded Lebesgue norms. In recent years, many results have been obtained, both in the Euclidean setting and in the Heisenberg groups, as well as for contact manifolds with bounded geometry. There are also some results for general Carnot groups; however, these do not cover the problem in its full generality.   In this paper, we consider a particular Carnot group, the so-called Cartan group (a free Carnot group, of step $3$ with $2$ generators), which provides a natural testing ground for these questions, since its step-three structure already exhibits several phenomena that do not occur in the Heisenberg groups. In this setting, we are able to prove global Poincaré and Sobolev-Gaffney inequalities for differential forms. With the aim of obtaining sharp estimates, we replace the de Rham complex of differential forms with the Rumin complex. The case $p>1$ is carried out after establishing an $L^p$-Hodge decomposition with homogeneous Sobolev classes. We are able to consider also the endpoint case $p=1$; however, as in Euclidean setting, when $p=1$, the operator we deal with provides only weak-type estimates which do not yield a Hodge decomposition analogous to the case $p>1$. Therefore, in this situation the proof follows a different approach, relying on a recent result proved in \cite{BT}.

ai.tldr: 本文在Cartan群（一种特殊的3步自由Carnot群）上，建立了微分形式的全局Poincaré和Sobolev-Gaffney不等式。通过引入Rumin复形并建立L^p-Hodge分解，获得了p>1时的精确估计，并对端点情形p=1给出了弱型估计。
ai.motivation: 在一般的Carnot群和更广泛的亚黎曼几何背景下，微分形式的Sobolev和Poincaré不等式问题尚未完全解决。Cartan群作为三步结构，其现象比Heisenberg群更复杂，为研究此类问题提供了理想的测试平台。
ai.method: 用Rumin复形替代de Rham复形以获取精确估计。对于p>1情形，通过建立具有齐次Sobolev类的L^p-Hodge分解来证明不等式；对于p=1端点情形，则依赖已有文献中的弱型估计结果，采用不同的证明路径。
ai.result: 在Cartan群上成功证明了微分形式的全局Poincaré和Sobolev-Gaffney不等式。对于p>1获得了完整的Hodge分解和强型估计；对于p=1获得了弱型估计，但无法得到类似的Hodge分解。
ai.help_to_user: 本文为亚黎曼几何中微分形式分析提供了具体的工具和结果，特别是三步Carnot群上的精确估计方法，可用于相关偏微分方程和几何分析问题的研究。

idea_spark.idea: 在更一般的Carnot群或具有类似分层结构的流形上，尝试建立类似的全局积分估计和不等式。
idea_spark.risk: 对于更高步数或更复杂结构的群，相应的复形构造和估计可能变得极其复杂，技术障碍较大。
idea_spark.inspiration: 本文展示了对端点情形（p=1）需要采用不同证明策略，这提示在临界情形下可能需要发展新的调和分析工具。

zh.result: 在Cartan群上成功证明了微分形式的全局Poincaré和Sobolev-Gaffney不等式。对于p>1获得了完整的Hodge分解和强型估计；对于p=1获得了弱型估计，但无法得到类似的Hodge分解。

en.tldr: This paper establishes global Poincaré and Sobolev-Gaffney inequalities for differential forms on the Cartan group, a specific free Carnot group of step 3. Sharp estimates for p>1 are obtained via an L^p-Hodge decomposition with homogeneous Sobolev classes using the Rumin complex, while weak-type estimates are provided for the endpoint case p=1.
en.motivation: Sobolev and Poincaré inequalities for differential forms in general Carnot groups and sub-Riemannian settings remain open problems. The Cartan group, with its step-three structure exhibiting phenomena absent in Heisenberg groups, serves as a natural testing ground for these questions.
en.method: The de Rham complex is replaced by the Rumin complex to obtain sharp estimates. For p>1, inequalities are proved by establishing an L^p-Hodge decomposition with homogeneous Sobolev classes. For the endpoint case p=1, the proof follows a different approach relying on weak-type estimates from existing literature.
en.result: Global Poincaré and Sobolev-Gaffney inequalities for differential forms are proved on the Cartan group. A full Hodge decomposition and strong estimates are obtained for p>1, while only weak-type estimates (without an analogous Hodge decomposition) are achieved for p=1.
en.help_to_user: This work provides concrete tools and results for the analysis of differential forms in sub-Riemannian geometry, particularly precise estimation methods on step-three Carnot groups, applicable to related PDE and geometric analysis problems.

idea_spark.idea: Attempt to establish similar global integral estimates and inequalities on more general Carnot groups or manifolds with analogous stratified structures.
idea_spark.risk: For groups with higher step or more complex structures, the corresponding complex construction and estimates may become extremely complicated, posing significant technical barriers.
idea_spark.inspiration: The paper demonstrates that different proof strategies are required for endpoint cases (p=1), suggesting that new harmonic analysis tools may need to be developed for critical cases.

papers.title: Quantitative measurements of biological/chemical concentrations using smartphone cameras
papers.summary: This paper presents a smartphone-based imaging system capable of quantifying the concentration of an assortment of biological/chemical assay samples. The main objective is to construct an image database which characterizes the relationship between color information and concentrations of the biological/chemical assay sample. For this aim, a designated optical setup combined with image processing and data analyzing techniques was implemented. A series of experiments conducted on selected assays, including fluorescein, RNA Mango, homogenized milk and yeast have demonstrated that the proposed system estimates the concentration of fluorescent materials and colloidal mixtures comparable to currently used commercial and laboratory instruments. Furthermore, by utilizing the camera and computational power of smartphones, eventual development can be directed toward extremely compact, inexpensive and portable analysis and diagnostic systems which will allow experiments and tests to be conducted in remote or impoverished areas.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于智能手机摄像头的成像系统，用于量化多种生物/化学检测样品的浓度。
ai.motivation: 构建一个表征颜色信息与样品浓度关系的图像数据库，以开发紧凑、廉价、便携的分析诊断系统，适用于偏远或贫困地区。
ai.method: 设计了一个特定的光学装置，结合图像处理和数据分析技术，对荧光素、RNA Mango、均质牛奶和酵母等选定检测物进行了一系列实验。
ai.result: 实验表明，该系统对荧光材料和胶体混合物浓度的估计结果与当前使用的商业和实验室仪器相当。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种利用智能手机硬件和计算能力进行现场定量检测的低成本方案。

idea_spark.idea: 利用智能手机摄像头建立颜色-浓度校准数据库，实现多种样品的快速定量。
idea_spark.risk: 环境光照条件、摄像头型号差异、样品基质干扰可能影响测量精度和普适性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将日常消费电子设备转化为专业科学仪器的思路，降低科学检测的门槛和成本。

en.tldr: This paper presents a smartphone camera-based imaging system for quantifying the concentration of various biological/chemical assay samples.
en.motivation: To build an image database characterizing the relationship between color information and sample concentration, aiming to develop compact, inexpensive, and portable analysis/diagnostic systems for use in remote or impoverished areas.
en.method: A designated optical setup combined with image processing and data analysis techniques was implemented. Experiments were conducted on selected assays including fluorescein, RNA Mango, homogenized milk, and yeast.
en.result: The proposed system estimates the concentration of fluorescent materials and colloidal mixtures with performance comparable to current commercial and laboratory instruments.
en.help_to_user: Provides a low-cost solution for on-site quantitative detection leveraging smartphone hardware and computational power.

idea_spark.idea: Establishing a color-concentration calibration database using smartphone cameras for rapid quantification of diverse samples.
idea_spark.risk: Measurement accuracy and generalizability may be affected by ambient light conditions, camera model variations, and sample matrix interference.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the transformation of everyday consumer electronics into professional scientific instruments, lowering the barrier and cost of scientific testing.

papers.title: Graph-Based Fraud Detection with Dual-Path Graph Filtering
papers.summary: Fraud detection on graph data can be viewed as a demanding task that requires distinguishing between different types of nodes. Because graph neural networks (GNNs) are naturally suited for processing information encoded in graph form through their message-passing operations, methods based on GNN models have increasingly attracted attention in the fraud detection domain. However, fraud graphs inherently exhibit relation camouflage, high heterophily, and class imbalance, causing most GNNs to underperform in fraud detection tasks. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Graph-Based Fraud Detection Model with Dual-Path Graph Filtering (DPF-GFD). DPF-GFD first applies a beta wavelet-based operator to the original graph to capture key structural patterns. It then constructs a similarity graph from distance-based node representations and applies an improved low-pass filter. The embeddings from the original and similarity graphs are fused through supervised representation learning to obtain node features, which are finally used by an ensemble tree model to assess the fraud risk of unlabeled nodes. Unlike existing single-graph smoothing approaches, DPF-GFD introduces a frequency-complementary dual-path filtering paradigm tailored for fraud detection, explicitly decoupling structural anomaly modeling and feature similarity modeling. This design enables more discriminative and stable node representations in highly heterophilous and imbalanced fraud graphs. Comprehensive experiments on four real-world financial fraud detection datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于双路径图过滤的图欺诈检测模型（DPF-GFD），通过互补的频率滤波范式，在高度异质性和不平衡的欺诈图中生成更具判别性和稳定性的节点表示。
ai.motivation: 欺诈图天然存在关系伪装、高异质性和类别不平衡问题，导致大多数图神经网络在欺诈检测任务中表现不佳。
ai.method: DPF-GFD首先在原始图上应用基于Beta小波的算子以捕获关键结构模式；然后从基于距离的节点表示构建相似图并应用改进的低通滤波器；最后通过监督表示学习融合两个图的嵌入，并使用集成树模型评估未标记节点的欺诈风险。
ai.result: 在四个真实世界金融欺诈检测数据集上的综合实验证明了所提方法的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种专门针对欺诈图异质性和不平衡特性设计的双路径图过滤框架，可提升欺诈检测的判别性和稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 将结构异常建模与特征相似性建模通过两个并行的图滤波路径进行显式解耦。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于构建有效的相似图，在特征稀疏或噪声极大的场景下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信号处理中的多频带/多路径滤波思想与图表示学习结合，以处理复杂图数据中的混合模式。

zh.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于双路径图过滤的图欺诈检测模型（DPF-GFD），通过互补的频率滤波范式，在高度异质性和不平衡的欺诈图中生成更具判别性和稳定性的节点表示。
zh.motivation: 欺诈图天然存在关系伪装、高异质性和类别不平衡问题，导致大多数图神经网络在欺诈检测任务中表现不佳。
zh.method: DPF-GFD首先在原始图上应用基于Beta小波的算子以捕获关键结构模式；然后从基于距离的节点表示构建相似图并应用改进的低通滤波器；最后通过监督表示学习融合两个图的嵌入，并使用集成树模型评估未标记节点的欺诈风险。
zh.result: 在四个真实世界金融欺诈检测数据集上的综合实验证明了所提方法的有效性。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种专门针对欺诈图异质性和不平衡特性设计的双路径图过滤框架，可提升欺诈检测的判别性和稳定性。

idea_spark.idea: 将结构异常建模与特征相似性建模通过两个并行的图滤波路径进行显式解耦。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于构建有效的相似图，在特征稀疏或噪声极大的场景下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将信号处理中的多频带/多路径滤波思想与图表示学习结合，以处理复杂图数据中的混合模式。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a Graph-Based Fraud Detection Model with Dual-Path Graph Filtering (DPF-GFD), which generates more discriminative and stable node representations in highly heterophilous and imbalanced fraud graphs through a frequency-complementary dual-path filtering paradigm.
en.motivation: Fraud graphs inherently exhibit relation camouflage, high heterophily, and class imbalance, causing most Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to underperform in fraud detection tasks.
en.method: DPF-GFD first applies a beta wavelet-based operator to the original graph to capture key structural patterns. It then constructs a similarity graph from distance-based node representations and applies an improved low-pass filter. The embeddings from both graphs are fused via supervised representation learning, and an ensemble tree model is used to assess the fraud risk of unlabeled nodes.
en.result: Comprehensive experiments on four real-world financial fraud detection datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.
en.help_to_user: Provides a dual-path graph filtering framework specifically designed for the heterophily and imbalance characteristics of fraud graphs, enhancing the discriminative power and stability of fraud detection.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly decouple structural anomaly modeling and feature similarity modeling through two parallel graph filtering paths.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on constructing an effective similarity graph, which may fail in scenarios with extremely sparse features or high noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the integration of multi-band/multi-path filtering ideas from signal processing with graph representation learning to handle mixed patterns in complex graph data.

papers.title: Uniform volume estimates and maximal functions on generalized Heisenberg-type groups
papers.summary: On generalized Heisenberg-type groups $\mathbb{G}(2n,m,\mathbb{U},\mathbb{W})$, we give uniform volume estimates for the ball defined by a large class of Carnot-Carathéodory distances, and establish weak (1, 1) $O(C^m \, n)$-estimates for associated centered Hardy-Littlewood maximal functions, extending the results in \cite{BLZ25}. As a by-product, we establish uniformly volume doubling property on Heisenberg groups for a class of left-invariant Riemannian metrics.

papers.title: Co-distilled attention guided masked image modeling with noisy teacher for self-supervised learning on medical images
papers.summary: Masked image modeling (MIM) is a highly effective self-supervised learning (SSL) approach to extract useful feature representations from unannotated data. Predominantly used random masking methods make SSL less effective for medical images due to the contextual similarity of neighboring patches, leading to information leakage and SSL simplification. Hierarchical shifted window (Swin) transformer, a highly effective approach for medical images cannot use advanced masking methods as it lacks a global [CLS] token. Hence, we introduced an attention guided masking mechanism for Swin within a co-distillation learning framework to selectively mask semantically co-occurring and discriminative patches, to reduce information leakage and increase the difficulty of SSL pretraining. However, attention guided masking inevitably reduces the diversity of attention heads, which negatively impacts downstream task performance. To address this, we for the first time, integrate a noisy teacher into the co-distillation framework (termed DAGMaN) that performs attentive masking while preserving high attention head diversity. We demonstrate the capability of DAGMaN on multiple tasks including full- and few-shot lung nodule classification, immunotherapy outcome prediction, tumor segmentation, and unsupervised organs clustering.

ai.tldr: 提出DAGMaN框架，在Swin Transformer中引入注意力引导掩码与噪声教师协同蒸馏，解决医学图像自监督学习中因上下文相似导致的信息泄露问题。
ai.motivation: 医学图像相邻块上下文相似，随机掩码导致信息泄露，使自监督学习简化；Swin Transformer缺乏全局[CLS]标记，无法使用高级掩码方法。
ai.method: 在协同蒸馏框架中为Swin设计注意力引导掩码机制，选择性掩码语义共现且具判别性的块；引入噪声教师保持注意力头多样性。
ai.result: 在肺结节分类（全样本/少样本）、免疫治疗结果预测、肿瘤分割、无监督器官聚类等多个任务上验证了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 为医学图像自监督学习提供了一种减少信息泄露、保持注意力多样性的掩码与蒸馏联合框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将噪声注入教师模型的注意力头，作为正则化手段来维持多样性，同时进行注意力引导的语义掩码。
idea_spark.risk: 注意力引导掩码可能过度依赖初始注意力分布，若教师噪声强度不当，可能破坏有益语义结构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索不同噪声模式（如结构化噪声）对注意力多样性的影响，或将该框架扩展至多模态医学图像预训练。

zh.tldr: 提出DAGMaN框架，在Swin Transformer中引入注意力引导掩码与噪声教师协同蒸馏，解决医学图像自监督学习中因上下文相似导致的信息泄露问题。

idea_spark.risk: 注意力引导掩码可能过度依赖初始注意力分布，若教师噪声强度不当，可能破坏有益语义结构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索不同噪声模式（如结构化噪声）对注意力多样性的影响，或将该框架扩展至多模态医学图像预训练。

en.tldr: Proposes DAGMaN, integrating attention-guided masking with a noisy teacher in a co-distillation framework for Swin Transformer, addressing information leakage in medical image SSL due to contextual similarity.
en.motivation: Random masking in SSL is less effective for medical images due to contextual similarity of neighboring patches, causing information leakage; Swin Transformer lacks a global [CLS] token, preventing use of advanced masking methods.
en.method: Designs an attention-guided masking mechanism within a co-distillation framework for Swin to selectively mask semantically co-occurring and discriminative patches; introduces a noisy teacher to preserve attention head diversity.
en.result: Demonstrates effectiveness on multiple tasks including full- and few-shot lung nodule classification, immunotherapy outcome prediction, tumor segmentation, and unsupervised organs clustering.
en.help_to_user: Provides a joint masking and distillation framework for medical image SSL that reduces information leakage while maintaining attention diversity.

idea_spark.idea: Inject noise into the teacher model's attention heads as a regularization means to maintain diversity while performing attention-guided semantic masking.
idea_spark.risk: Attention-guided masking may over-rely on initial attention distribution; inappropriate teacher noise strength could disrupt beneficial semantic structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore the impact of different noise patterns (e.g., structured noise) on attention diversity, or extend the framework to multimodal medical image pretraining.

papers.title: Computing the free energy of quantum Coulomb gases and molecules via quantum Gibbs sampling
papers.summary: We develop a quantum algorithm for estimating the free energy as well as the total Gibbs state of interacting quantum Coulomb gases and molecular systems in dimensions $d \in \{2,3\}$ at finite temperature. These systems lie beyond the reach of existing methods due to their singular interactions and infinite-dimensional Hilbert space structure. First, we show that the free energy of the full many-body Hamiltonian can be approximated by that of the same Hamiltonian with a finite-rank low-energy truncation of the interaction, with an explicit error bound polynomial in the particle number. This reduces the problem to a controlled finite-rank perturbation problem. Second, we introduce a quantum Gibbs sampling scheme tailored to this truncated system, based on a class of quantum Markov semigroups. Our main analytical result establishes that the associated generator has a strictly positive spectral gap for every truncation, implying exponential convergence to the target Gibbs state. This provides, to our knowledge, the first rigorous mixing-time guarantee for Gibbs sampling in a Coulomb interacting continuous-variable quantum system. Finally, we give an explicit quantum circuit implementation of the dynamics and derive an end-to-end complexity bound for approximating the free energy and the Gibbs state itself. Our results provide a mathematically rigorous route to quantum algorithms for free energy estimation in interacting quantum systems, without relying on classical approximations such as the Born-Oppenheimer reduction.

ai.tldr: 开发了一种量子算法，用于估计有限温度下二维和三维量子库仑气体与分子系统的自由能和总吉布斯态。通过有限秩截断和量子吉布斯采样方案，首次为库仑相互作用的连续变量量子系统提供了严格的混合时间保证。
ai.motivation: 现有的方法无法处理具有奇异相互作用和无限维希尔伯特空间结构的量子库仑气体与分子系统，需要新的量子算法来精确计算其自由能和吉布斯态。
ai.method: 首先，将全多体哈密顿量的自由能近似为具有有限秩低能截断相互作用的哈密顿量的自由能，误差界与粒子数呈多项式关系。其次，针对截断系统设计基于量子马尔可夫半群的量子吉布斯采样方案，并证明其生成元具有严格正谱隙，确保指数收敛到目标吉布斯态。最后，给出动力学的显式量子电路实现。
ai.result: 为库仑相互作用的连续变量量子系统的吉布斯采样提供了首个严格的混合时间保证，并推导了近似自由能和吉布斯态的端到端复杂度界。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种数学上严格的量子算法路径，用于估计相互作用量子系统的自由能，无需依赖玻恩-奥本海默近似等经典近似。

idea_spark.idea: 将无限维问题转化为有限秩扰动问题，并结合具有严格谱隙保证的量子动力学进行采样。
idea_spark.risk: 截断误差在极端参数下可能累积；量子电路实现的实际噪声影响需进一步评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为连续变量量子系统的吉布斯采样提供了首个严格收敛性证明，启发了将经典统计力学中的截断技术与量子动力学结合的新思路。
