en.tldr: Proposes a heartbeat-driven autonomous thinking activity scheduling mechanism that enables LLM agents to perform proactive, adaptive, and continuous self-regulation, dynamically managing cognitive modules and optimizing strategies.
en.motivation: Existing LLM agents often rely on fixed pipelines or failure-triggered reflection, leading to rigid, reactive behavior with limited adaptability and efficiency. A more human-cognition-like proactive regulation mechanism is needed.
en.method: Introduces a periodic 'heartbeat' mechanism to orchestrate cognitive modules (e.g., Planner, Critic, Recaller, Dreamer); employs a meta-learning strategy for continual policy adaptation using historical interaction logs; supports dynamic addition/removal of modules.
en.result: Evaluation shows the approach effectively learns to schedule cognitive activities based on historical data and can autonomously integrate new thinking modules, achieving adaptive and continuous self-regulation.
en.help_to_user: Provides an extensible scheduling framework for building more flexible, adaptive, human-cognition-like LLM agents, supporting dynamic module management and continual policy optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Abstract biological rhythms (e.g., heartbeat, respiration) as temporal scheduling signals to drive periodic self-reflection and strategy adjustment in AI systems.
idea_spark.risk: Matching heartbeat frequency to task rhythms requires fine-tuning; meta-learning policies may converge slowly or get stuck in local optima.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human 'intermittent reflection,' using programmed rhythms instead of fixed triggers to enable more natural autonomous thinking scheduling.

papers.title: COEVO: Co-Evolutionary Framework for Joint Functional Correctness and PPA Optimization in LLM-Based RTL Generation
papers.summary: LLM-based RTL code generation methods increasingly target both functional correctness and PPA quality, yet existing approaches universally decouple the two objectives, optimizing PPA only after correctness is fully achieved. Whether through sequential multi-agent pipelines, evolutionary search with binary correctness gates, or hierarchical reward dependencies, partially correct but architecturally promising candidates are systematically discarded. Moreover, existing methods reduce the multi-objective PPA space to a single scalar fitness, obscuring the trade-offs among area, delay, and power. To address these limitations, we propose COEVO, a co-evolutionary framework that unifies correctness and PPA optimization within a single evolutionary loop. COEVO formulates correctness as a continuous co-optimization dimension alongside area, delay, and power, enabled by an enhanced testbench that provides fine-grained scoring and detailed diagnostic feedback. An adaptive correctness gate with annealing allows PPA-promising but partially correct candidates to guide the search toward jointly optimal solutions. To preserve the full PPA trade-off structure, COEVO employs four-dimensional Pareto-based non-dominated sorting with configurable intra-level sorting, replacing scalar fitness without manual weight tuning. Evaluated on VerilogEval 2.0 and RTLLM 2.0, COEVO achieves 97.5\% and 94.5\% Pass@1 with GPT-5.4-mini, surpassing all agentic baselines across four LLM backbones, while attaining the best PPA on 43 out of 49 synthesizable RTLLM designs.

ai.tldr: COEVO是一个协同进化框架，在单个进化循环中统一优化RTL代码的功能正确性和PPA（面积、延迟、功耗）质量，避免现有方法丢弃部分正确但有架构潜力的候选方案。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM生成RTL的方法将功能正确性和PPA优化解耦，先确保完全正确再优化PPA，导致部分正确但有PPA潜力的设计被丢弃，且PPA多目标被简化为单一标量，掩盖了面积、延迟、功耗间的权衡。
ai.method: 将正确性作为与面积、延迟、功耗并列的连续优化维度；使用增强测试平台提供细粒度评分和诊断反馈；采用带退火的自适应正确性门控，允许部分正确的候选引导搜索；使用四维帕累托非支配排序保留完整的PPA权衡结构。
ai.result: 在VerilogEval 2.0和RTLLM 2.0基准上，使用GPT-5.4-mini分别达到97.5%和94.5%的Pass@1，超越所有智能体基线，并在49个可综合RTLLM设计中的43个上获得最佳PPA。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要同时优化RTL功能正确性和PPA质量的研究者或工程师提供了一种统一的框架，避免了顺序优化导致的有潜力设计被过早丢弃的问题。

idea_spark.idea: 在其他需要同时满足硬性约束（如正确性、安全性）和软性优化目标（如性能、成本）的自动生成任务中，借鉴连续协同优化框架。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于增强测试平台提供细粒度反馈，在缺乏此类反馈或反馈成本高的领域可能难以直接应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将传统上二值化的“合格/不合格”门控转化为可引导搜索过程的连续信号，从而更充分地探索解空间。

zh.tldr: COEVO是一个协同进化框架，在单个进化循环中统一优化RTL代码的功能正确性和PPA（面积、延迟、功耗）质量，避免现有方法丢弃部分正确但有架构潜力的候选方案。
zh.motivation: 现有LLM生成RTL的方法将功能正确性和PPA优化解耦，先确保完全正确再优化PPA，导致部分正确但有PPA潜力的设计被丢弃，且PPA多目标被简化为单一标量，掩盖了面积、延迟、功耗间的权衡。
zh.method: 将正确性作为与面积、延迟、功耗并列的连续优化维度；使用增强测试平台提供细粒度评分和诊断反馈；采用带退火的自适应正确性门控，允许部分正确的候选引导搜索；使用四维帕累托非支配排序保留完整的PPA权衡结构。
zh.result: 在VerilogEval 2.0和RTLLM 2.0基准上，使用GPT-5.4-mini分别达到97.5%和94.5%的Pass@1，超越所有智能体基线，并在49个可综合RTLLM设计中的43个上获得最佳PPA。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要同时优化RTL功能正确性和PPA质量的研究者或工程师提供了一种统一的框架，避免了顺序优化导致的有潜力设计被过早丢弃的问题。

idea_spark.idea: 在其他需要同时满足硬性约束（如正确性、安全性）和软性优化目标（如性能、成本）的自动生成任务中，借鉴连续协同优化框架。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于增强测试平台提供细粒度反馈，在缺乏此类反馈或反馈成本高的领域可能难以直接应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将传统上二值化的“合格/不合格”门控转化为可引导搜索过程的连续信号，从而更充分地探索解空间。

en.tldr: COEVO is a co-evolutionary framework that unifies functional correctness and PPA (area, delay, power) optimization for LLM-based RTL generation within a single evolutionary loop, preventing the discard of partially correct but architecturally promising candidates.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-based RTL generation methods decouple functional correctness and PPA optimization, ensuring full correctness first, which discards partially correct designs with PPA potential and reduces multi-objective PPA to a single scalar, obscuring trade-offs.
en.method: Formulates correctness as a continuous optimization dimension alongside area, delay, and power; uses an enhanced testbench for fine-grained scoring and diagnostic feedback; employs an adaptive correctness gate with annealing; replaces scalar fitness with four-dimensional Pareto-based non-dominated sorting to preserve trade-off structure.
en.result: Achieves 97.5% and 94.5% Pass@1 on VerilogEval 2.0 and RTLLM 2.0 benchmarks using GPT-5.4-mini, surpassing all agentic baselines and attaining the best PPA on 43 out of 49 synthesizable RTLLM designs.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework for researchers or engineers needing to jointly optimize RTL functional correctness and PPA quality, avoiding premature discard of promising designs due to sequential optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Adapt the continuous co-optimization framework to other automated generation tasks requiring simultaneous satisfaction of hard constraints (e.g., correctness, safety) and soft optimization objectives (e.g., performance, cost).
idea_spark.risk: Relies on an enhanced testbench for fine-grained feedback, which may be difficult to apply directly in domains lacking such feedback or where it is costly to obtain.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of how to transform traditionally binary pass/fail gates into continuous signals that can guide the search process more effectively, enabling fuller exploration of the solution space.

papers.title: Improved Multiscale Structural Mapping with Supervertex Vision Transformer for the Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Neurodegeneration
papers.summary: Alzheimer's disease (AD) confirmation often relies on positron emission tomography (PET) or cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis, which are costly and invasive. Consequently, structural MRI biomarkers such as cortical thickness (CT) are widely used for non-invasive AD screening. Multiscale structural mapping (MSSM) was recently proposed to integrate gray-white matter contrasts (GWCs) with CT from a single T1-weighted MRI (T1w) scan. Building on this framework, we propose MSSM+, together with surface supervertex mapping (SSVM) and a Supervertex Vision Transformer (SV-ViT). 3D T1w images from individuals with AD and cognitively normal (CN) controls were analyzed. MSSM+ extends MSSM by incorporating sulcal depth and cortical curvature at the vertex level. SSVM partitions the cortical surface into supervertices (surface patches) that effectively represent inter- and intra-regional spatial relationships. SV-ViT is a Vision Transformer architecture operating on these supervertices, enabling anatomically informed learning from surface mesh representations. Compared with MSSM, MSSM+ identified more spatially extensive and statistically significant group differences between AD and CN. In AD vs. CN classification, MSSM+ achieved a 3%p higher area under the precision-recall curve than MSSM. Vendor-specific analyses further demonstrated reduced signal variability and consistently improved classification performance across MR manufacturers relative to CT, GWCs, and MSSM. These findings suggest that MSSM+ combined with SV-ViT is a promising MRI-based imaging marker for AD detection prior to CSF/PET confirmation.

ai.tldr: 提出MSSM+框架与Supervertex Vision Transformer，通过整合皮层表面多尺度特征改进阿尔茨海默病的结构MRI检测。
ai.motivation: PET和脑脊液检测成本高且有创，需要开发非侵入性、基于结构MRI的可靠生物标志物。
ai.method: 扩展MSSM，加入沟深和皮层曲率顶点特征；使用表面超顶点映射划分皮层；设计SV-ViT在超顶点上进行解剖结构感知学习。
ai.result: MSSM+比MSSM识别出更广泛显著的组间差异，分类AUC提升3%；跨MRI厂商信号变异性降低，性能稳定优于CT、GWC和MSSM。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种将多尺度皮层表面特征与视觉Transformer结合的新框架，可用于神经退行性疾病的MRI生物标志物开发。

idea_spark.idea: 将皮层表面划分为解剖学意义的超顶点作为ViT输入，实现结构感知的表示学习。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖T1加权图像质量与表面重建精度；超顶点划分的尺度需要针对不同任务优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将视觉Transformer适配于非欧几里得表面数据，结合多尺度几何特征提升模型解剖可解释性。

zh.tldr: 提出MSSM+框架与Supervertex Vision Transformer，通过整合皮层表面多尺度特征改进阿尔茨海默病的结构MRI检测。
zh.motivation: PET和脑脊液检测成本高且有创，需要开发非侵入性、基于结构MRI的可靠生物标志物。
zh.method: 扩展MSSM，加入沟深和皮层曲率顶点特征；使用表面超顶点映射划分皮层；设计SV-ViT在超顶点上进行解剖结构感知学习。
zh.result: MSSM+比MSSM识别出更广泛显著的组间差异，分类AUC提升3%；跨MRI厂商信号变异性降低，性能稳定优于CT、GWC和MSSM。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种将多尺度皮层表面特征与视觉Transformer结合的新框架，可用于神经退行性疾病的MRI生物标志物开发。

idea_spark.idea: 将皮层表面划分为解剖学意义的超顶点作为ViT输入，实现结构感知的表示学习。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖T1加权图像质量与表面重建精度；超顶点划分的尺度需要针对不同任务优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将视觉Transformer适配于非欧几里得表面数据，结合多尺度几何特征提升模型解剖可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes MSSM+ framework with Supervertex Vision Transformer to improve Alzheimer's disease detection from structural MRI by integrating multiscale cortical surface features.
en.motivation: PET and CSF analysis are costly and invasive, necessitating non-invasive, reliable structural MRI biomarkers for AD screening.
en.method: Extends MSSM with sulcal depth and cortical curvature at vertex level; employs surface supervertex mapping to partition cortex; designs SV-ViT for anatomically informed learning on supervertices.
en.result: MSSM+ identifies more extensive and significant group differences than MSSM, achieves 3% higher AUC in classification, and shows reduced vendor variability with consistent improvement over CT, GWC, and MSSM.
en.help_to_user: Offers a novel framework combining multiscale cortical surface features with Vision Transformer for developing MRI-based biomarkers in neurodegenerative diseases.

idea_spark.idea: Partition cortical surface into anatomically meaningful supervertices as ViT input for structure-aware representation learning.
idea_spark.risk: Method depends on T1w image quality and surface reconstruction accuracy; supervertex scale requires task-specific optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires adapting Vision Transformers to non-Euclidean surface data, integrating multiscale geometric features to enhance anatomical interpretability.

papers.title: Disentangle-then-Refine: LLM-Guided Decoupling and Structure-Aware Refinement for Graph Contrastive Learning
papers.summary: Conventional Graph Contrastive Learning (GCL) on Text-Attributed Graphs (TAGs) relies on blind stochastic augmentations, inadvertently entangling task-relevant signals with noise. We propose SDM-SCR, a robust framework anchored in Approximate Orthogonal Decomposition. First, the Semantic Decoupling Module (SDM) leverages the instruction-following capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to actively parse raw attributes into asymmetric, task-oriented signal and noise views. This shifts the paradigm from random perturbation to semantic-aware disentanglement. Subsequently, Semantic Consistency Regularization (SCR) exploits the spectral observation that semantic signals are topologically smooth while residual noise is high-frequency. SCR functions as a selective spectral filter, enforcing consistency only on the signal subspace to eliminate LLM hallucinations without over-smoothing. This ``Disentangle-then-Refine'' mechanism ensures rigorous signal purification. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SDM-SCR achieves SOTA performance in accuracy and efficiency.

ai.tldr: 提出SDM-SCR框架，利用LLM主动解耦文本属性图中的信号与噪声，并通过谱一致性正则化精炼信号，提升图对比学习性能。
ai.motivation: 传统图对比学习在文本属性图上依赖随机增强，导致任务相关信号与噪声纠缠，影响学习效果。
ai.method: 1. 语义解耦模块（SDM）利用LLM指令跟随能力，将原始属性解析为不对称的任务导向信号视图和噪声视图。2. 语义一致性正则化（SCR）基于谱观察，强制信号子空间一致性，作为选择性谱滤波器消除LLM幻觉且避免过平滑。
ai.result: 大量实验表明SDM-SCR在准确性和效率上达到最先进性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理文本属性图提供了一种从随机扰动转向语义感知解耦的图对比学习新范式，显著提升信号纯度。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM的指令解析能力用于主动构建对比视图，替代随机增强；利用信号与噪声的谱特性差异进行选择性正则化。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的解析质量与计算成本；谱假设在复杂图结构中可能不总是成立。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索其他模态（如图像、多模态）的属性解耦；将解耦思想与不同图神经网络架构结合。

zh.tldr: 提出SDM-SCR框架，利用LLM主动解耦文本属性图中的信号与噪声，并通过谱一致性正则化精炼信号，提升图对比学习性能。
zh.motivation: 传统图对比学习在文本属性图上依赖随机增强，导致任务相关信号与噪声纠缠，影响学习效果。

zh.result: 大量实验表明SDM-SCR在准确性和效率上达到最先进性能。
zh.help_to_user: 为处理文本属性图提供了一种从随机扰动转向语义感知解耦的图对比学习新范式，显著提升信号纯度。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM的指令解析能力用于主动构建对比视图，替代随机增强；利用信号与噪声的谱特性差异进行选择性正则化。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的解析质量与计算成本；谱假设在复杂图结构中可能不总是成立。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索其他模态（如图像、多模态）的属性解耦；将解耦思想与不同图神经网络架构结合。

en.tldr: Proposes SDM-SCR, a framework that uses LLMs to actively disentangle signals from noise in Text-Attributed Graphs and refines signals via spectral consistency regularization, enhancing Graph Contrastive Learning performance.
en.motivation: Conventional Graph Contrastive Learning on Text-Attributed Graphs relies on blind stochastic augmentations, inadvertently entangling task-relevant signals with noise, impairing learning effectiveness.
en.method: 1. Semantic Decoupling Module (SDM) leverages LLM instruction-following to parse raw attributes into asymmetric, task-oriented signal and noise views. 2. Semantic Consistency Regularization (SCR) enforces consistency only on the signal subspace based on spectral observations, acting as a selective spectral filter to eliminate LLM hallucinations without over-smoothing.
en.result: Extensive experiments demonstrate that SDM-SCR achieves state-of-the-art performance in both accuracy and efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new paradigm for Graph Contrastive Learning on Text-Attributed Graphs, shifting from random perturbation to semantic-aware disentanglement, significantly improving signal purity.

idea_spark.idea: Using LLM's instruction-following capability to actively construct contrastive views instead of random augmentations; leveraging spectral differences between signals and noise for selective regularization.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on LLM parsing quality and computational cost; spectral assumptions may not always hold in complex graph structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore attribute decoupling for other modalities (e.g., images, multimodal); integrate the disentanglement idea with different GNN architectures.

papers.title: Pushing the Limits of On-Device Streaming ASR: A Compact, High-Accuracy English Model for Low-Latency Inference
papers.summary: Deploying high-quality automatic speech recognition (ASR) on edge devices requires models that jointly optimize accuracy, latency, and memory footprint while operating entirely on CPU without GPU acceleration. We conduct a systematic empirical study of state-of-the-art ASR architectures, encompassing encoder-decoder, transducer, and LLM-based paradigms, evaluated across batch, chunked, and streaming inference modes. Through a comprehensive benchmark of over 50 configurations spanning OpenAI Whisper, NVIDIA Nemotron, Parakeet TDT, Canary, Conformer Transducer, and Qwen3-ASR, we identify NVIDIA's Nemotron Speech Streaming as the strongest candidate for real-time English streaming on resource-constrained hardware. We then re-implement the complete streaming inference pipeline in ONNX Runtime and conduct a controlled evaluation of multiple post-training quantization strategies, including importance-weighted k-quant, mixed-precision schemes, and round-to-nearest quantization, combined with graph-level operator fusion. These optimizations reduce the model from 2.47 GB to as little as 0.67 GB while maintaining word error rate (WER) within 1% absolute of the full-precision PyTorch baseline. Our recommended configuration, the int4 k-quant variant, achieves 8.20% average streaming WER across eight standard benchmarks, running comfortably faster than real-time on CPU with 0.56 s algorithmic latency, establishing a new quality-efficiency Pareto point for on-device streaming ASR.

ai.tldr: 本研究系统评估了多种ASR架构，确定NVIDIA Nemotron Speech Streaming为最佳候选，并通过ONNX Runtime优化和量化策略，将模型压缩至0.67GB，在CPU上实现低延迟、高精度的实时英语流式语音识别。
ai.motivation: 在边缘设备上部署高质量ASR需要模型在仅使用CPU、无GPU加速的条件下，同时优化精度、延迟和内存占用。
ai.method: 对超过50种ASR配置进行基准测试，选定最佳架构后，在ONNX Runtime中重新实现完整的流式推理管道，并评估多种训练后量化策略（如重要性加权k-quant、混合精度）与算子融合技术。
ai.result: 优化将模型从2.47GB压缩至最小0.67GB，词错误率（WER）与全精度PyTorch基线相比绝对差异保持在1%以内。推荐的int4 k-quant变体在八个标准基准测试中平均流式WER为8.20%，在CPU上以0.56秒算法延迟实现超实时运行。
ai.help_to_user: 为在资源受限硬件上部署实时英语流式ASR提供了经过全面基准测试和优化的完整解决方案与具体配置建议。

idea_spark.idea: 将经过严格基准测试选定的高性能ASR架构，与先进的训练后量化及运行时优化技术深度结合，专门针对CPU边缘部署场景进行端到端优化。
idea_spark.risk: 优化策略和性能结果可能高度依赖于特定的模型架构（Nemotron）和英语语音数据，在其他语言或架构上迁移效果未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他需要在边缘设备上部署的实时AI任务（如关键词检测、语音合成）提供了一套从架构选择、量化压缩到运行时优化的完整方法论参考。

zh.tldr: 本研究系统评估了多种ASR架构，确定NVIDIA Nemotron Speech Streaming为最佳候选，并通过ONNX Runtime优化和量化策略，将模型压缩至0.67GB，在CPU上实现低延迟、高精度的实时英语流式语音识别。
zh.motivation: 在边缘设备上部署高质量ASR需要模型在仅使用CPU、无GPU加速的条件下，同时优化精度、延迟和内存占用。
zh.method: 对超过50种ASR配置进行基准测试，选定最佳架构后，在ONNX Runtime中重新实现完整的流式推理管道，并评估多种训练后量化策略（如重要性加权k-quant、混合精度）与算子融合技术。
zh.result: 优化将模型从2.47GB压缩至最小0.67GB，词错误率（WER）与全精度PyTorch基线相比绝对差异保持在1%以内。推荐的int4 k-quant变体在八个标准基准测试中平均流式WER为8.20%，在CPU上以0.56秒算法延迟实现超实时运行。
zh.help_to_user: 为在资源受限硬件上部署实时英语流式ASR提供了经过全面基准测试和优化的完整解决方案与具体配置建议。

idea_spark.idea: 将经过严格基准测试选定的高性能ASR架构，与先进的训练后量化及运行时优化技术深度结合，专门针对CPU边缘部署场景进行端到端优化。
idea_spark.risk: 优化策略和性能结果可能高度依赖于特定的模型架构（Nemotron）和英语语音数据，在其他语言或架构上迁移效果未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他需要在边缘设备上部署的实时AI任务（如关键词检测、语音合成）提供了一套从架构选择、量化压缩到运行时优化的完整方法论参考。

en.tldr: This study systematically evaluates various ASR architectures, identifies NVIDIA Nemotron Speech Streaming as the optimal candidate, and through ONNX Runtime optimization and quantization strategies, compresses the model to 0.67GB, achieving low-latency, high-accuracy real-time English streaming speech recognition on CPU.
en.motivation: Deploying high-quality ASR on edge devices requires models that jointly optimize accuracy, latency, and memory footprint while operating entirely on CPU without GPU acceleration.
en.method: Benchmarks over 50 ASR configurations, selects the best architecture, re-implements the complete streaming inference pipeline in ONNX Runtime, and evaluates multiple post-training quantization strategies (e.g., importance-weighted k-quant, mixed-precision) combined with operator fusion.
en.result: Optimizations reduce the model from 2.47GB to as little as 0.67GB while maintaining word error rate (WER) within 1% absolute of the full-precision PyTorch baseline. The recommended int4 k-quant variant achieves 8.20% average streaming WER across eight standard benchmarks, running faster than real-time on CPU with 0.56 s algorithmic latency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a comprehensively benchmarked and optimized end-to-end solution with specific configuration recommendations for deploying real-time English streaming ASR on resource-constrained hardware.

idea_spark.idea: Deep integration of a high-performance ASR architecture selected through rigorous benchmarking with advanced post-training quantization and runtime optimization techniques, tailored for end-to-end optimization in CPU-edge deployment scenarios.
idea_spark.risk: The optimization strategies and performance results may be highly specific to the particular model architecture (Nemotron) and English speech data; transferability to other languages or architectures is unknown.
idea_spark.inspiration: Provides a complete methodology reference—from architecture selection and quantization compression to runtime optimization—for other real-time AI tasks requiring edge deployment, such as keyword spotting or speech synthesis.

papers.title: IG-Search: Step-Level Information Gain Rewards for Search-Augmented Reasoning
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning has emerged as an effective paradigm for training large language models to perform search-augmented reasoning. However, existing approaches rely on trajectory-level rewards that cannot distinguish precise search queries from vague or redundant ones within a rollout group, and collapse to a near-zero gradient signal whenever every sampled trajectory fails. In this paper, we propose IG-Search, a reinforcement learning framework that introduces a step-level reward based on Information Gain (IG). For each search step, IG measures how much the retrieved documents improve the model's confidence in the gold answer relative to a counterfactual baseline of random documents, thereby reflecting the effectiveness of the underlying search query. This signal is fed back to the corresponding search-query tokens via per-token advantage modulation in GRPO, enabling fine-grained, step-level credit assignment within a rollout. Unlike prior step-level methods that require either externally annotated intermediate supervision or shared environment states across trajectories, IG-Search derives its signals from the policy's own generation probabilities, requiring no intermediate annotations beyond standard question-answer pairs. Experiments on seven single-hop and multi-hop QA benchmarks demonstrate that IG-Search achieves an average EM of 0.430 with Qwen2.5-3B, outperforming the strongest trajectory-level baseline (MR-Search) by 1.6 points and the step-level method GiGPO by 0.9 points on average across benchmarks, with particularly pronounced gains on multi-hop reasoning tasks. Despite introducing a dense step-level signal, IG-Search adds only ~6.4% to per-step training wall-clock time over the trajectory-level baseline and leaves inference latency unchanged, while still providing a meaningful gradient signal even when every sampled trajectory answers incorrectly.

ai.tldr: IG-Search 是一个强化学习框架，通过基于信息增益的步级奖励来训练大语言模型进行搜索增强推理，解决了轨迹级奖励信号稀疏和模糊的问题。
ai.motivation: 现有的搜索增强推理方法依赖轨迹级奖励，无法区分一个 rollout 组内精确搜索查询与模糊冗余查询，且当所有采样轨迹都失败时梯度信号近乎为零。
ai.method: 提出步级信息增益奖励。对于每个搜索步骤，IG 衡量检索到的文档相对于随机文档的基线，对模型在正确答案上置信度的提升程度。该信号通过 GRPO 中的逐令牌优势调制反馈给对应的搜索查询令牌，实现细粒度的步级信用分配。
ai.result: 在七个单跳和多跳 QA 基准测试中，IG-Search 使用 Qwen2.5-3B 实现了平均 EM 0.430，优于最强的轨迹级基线（MR-Search）1.6 个百分点，优于步级方法 GiGPO 0.9 个百分点，在多跳推理任务上增益尤其显著。
ai.help_to_user: 为训练大语言模型进行更精确、高效的搜索增强推理提供了一种无需中间标注、计算开销低的步级奖励方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型自身生成概率（相对于随机基线）来量化每个搜索步骤的信息贡献，避免了对外部中间监督或跨轨迹共享环境状态的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 信息增益的计算依赖于模型对正确答案的置信度，在答案模糊或模型校准不佳的场景下可能不可靠。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何利用模型内部状态（如概率分布）来构建自监督的、任务相关的中间奖励信号，以改善长序列决策中的信用分配问题。

zh.tldr: IG-Search 是一个强化学习框架，通过基于信息增益的步级奖励来训练大语言模型进行搜索增强推理，解决了轨迹级奖励信号稀疏和模糊的问题。
zh.motivation: 现有的搜索增强推理方法依赖轨迹级奖励，无法区分一个 rollout 组内精确搜索查询与模糊冗余查询，且当所有采样轨迹都失败时梯度信号近乎为零。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型自身生成概率（相对于随机基线）来量化每个搜索步骤的信息贡献，避免了对外部中间监督或跨轨迹共享环境状态的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 信息增益的计算依赖于模型对正确答案的置信度，在答案模糊或模型校准不佳的场景下可能不可靠。

en.tldr: IG-Search is a reinforcement learning framework that trains large language models for search-augmented reasoning using step-level rewards based on Information Gain, addressing sparse and ambiguous signals from trajectory-level rewards.
en.motivation: Existing approaches for search-augmented reasoning rely on trajectory-level rewards, which cannot distinguish precise search queries from vague or redundant ones within a rollout group and collapse to near-zero gradient signals when all sampled trajectories fail.
en.method: Proposes a step-level Information Gain reward. For each search step, IG measures how much the retrieved documents improve the model's confidence in the gold answer relative to a counterfactual baseline of random documents. This signal is fed back to the corresponding search-query tokens via per-token advantage modulation in GRPO, enabling fine-grained, step-level credit assignment.
en.result: Experiments on seven single-hop and multi-hop QA benchmarks show that IG-Search achieves an average EM of 0.430 with Qwen2.5-3B, outperforming the strongest trajectory-level baseline (MR-Search) by 1.6 points and the step-level method GiGPO by 0.9 points on average, with particularly pronounced gains on multi-hop reasoning tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method for training LLMs to perform more precise and efficient search-augmented reasoning, using a step-level reward that requires no intermediate annotations and adds minimal computational overhead.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages the model's own generation probabilities (relative to a random baseline) to quantify the information contribution of each search step, eliminating the need for externally annotated intermediate supervision or shared environment states across trajectories.
idea_spark.risk: The calculation of Information Gain relies on the model's confidence in the correct answer, which may be unreliable in scenarios with ambiguous answers or poor model calibration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires thinking about how to use a model's internal state (e.g., probability distributions) to construct self-supervised, task-relevant intermediate reward signals to improve credit assignment in long-sequence decision-making.

papers.title: Entropy considerations in Many-Body Gravity and General Relativity, and the impact on cosmic inflation
papers.summary: Many body gravity (MBG) is a novel modified theory of gravity formulated in a 5-D space-time-temperature framework, in which the variation in temperature is recast as a variation in the 5-D metric. Previous work on MBG has shown that it can reproduce galaxy rotation curves, radial acceleration relation and the weak gravitational lensing of the bullet cluster, without the inclusion of dark matter. In this work we show that MBG can reproduce cosmic inflation, and in the process, analyze fundamental relations between interaction, time and gravity. To analyze cosmic inflation using interacting massless scalar fields, we first analyze theoretically a hypothetical universe with a single massive particle, or a collection of non-interacting massive particles. A quantitative relation between time and interaction is developed using Quantum Field Theory (QFT), which suggests that the notion of time becomes ill-defined for such a universe. The mass terms in MBG and General Relativity cause a discrepancy with the QFT results. An interacting massless scalar field then becomes a necessity to resolve the issue at the onset of inflation. However, the entropic terms in the MBG field equations are seen to be consistent with the QFT results and further accelerate inflation. The slow-roll condition is shown to be a natural consequence of the Euler-Lagrange equations of motion governing the massless scalar field in 5-D space-time-temperature, during the early phase of inflation. Finally, the MBG field equations are solved in the context of a Friedmann metric, leading to inflation. The matter era is also investigated.

ai.tldr: 本文在五维时空温度框架下的多体引力理论中，分析了熵对宇宙暴胀的影响，并建立了相互作用与时间之间的定量关系。
ai.motivation: 多体引力理论此前已能在不引入暗物质的情况下解释星系旋转曲线等观测现象，本研究旨在探索该理论能否再现宇宙暴胀过程，并深入分析相互作用、时间与引力之间的基本关系。
ai.method: 首先利用量子场论分析假设的仅含单个或多个无相互作用大质量粒子的宇宙，建立时间与相互作用的定量关系；随后在五维时空温度框架下，通过欧拉-拉格朗日运动方程研究无相互作用质量标量场，并将多体引力场方程置于弗里德曼度规中求解。
ai.result: 研究发现，多体引力理论能够再现宇宙暴胀，其场方程中的熵项与量子场论结果一致且能进一步加速暴胀；暴胀早期的慢滚条件成为五维框架下质量标量场运动方程的自然结果。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解修改引力理论如何在不依赖暗物质的前提下统一描述星系尺度现象与早期宇宙暴胀提供了新视角，并揭示了熵在引力动力学中的潜在作用。

idea_spark.idea: 在修改引力理论中引入熵驱动机制来解释宇宙加速膨胀，或将高维时空温度框架应用于其他早期宇宙相变问题。
idea_spark.risk: 理论高度形式化且缺乏直接观测检验，五维物理诠释可能存在争议，与现有宇宙学模型的数值拟合尚待深入。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从热力学与信息角度重构引力场方程，或探索早期宇宙中量子纠缠与时空涌现的关联模型。

en.tldr: This work analyzes the impact of entropy on cosmic inflation within the Many-Body Gravity framework formulated in 5-D space-time-temperature, and establishes a quantitative relation between interaction and time.
en.motivation: Since Many-Body Gravity has previously reproduced galaxy rotation curves and other phenomena without dark matter, this study aims to investigate whether it can also reproduce cosmic inflation, and to analyze fundamental relations between interaction, time, and gravity.
en.method: First, Quantum Field Theory is used to analyze a hypothetical universe with a single or non-interacting massive particles, developing a quantitative relation between time and interaction. Then, the Euler-Lagrange equations for a massless scalar field in 5-D space-time-temperature are studied, and the MBG field equations are solved within a Friedmann metric.
en.result: MBG is shown to reproduce cosmic inflation; the entropic terms in its field equations are consistent with QFT results and further accelerate inflation. The slow-roll condition emerges naturally from the equations of motion for the massless scalar field in the early inflationary phase.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel perspective on how modified gravity can unify galaxy-scale phenomena and early-universe inflation without dark matter, highlighting the potential role of entropy in gravitational dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing entropy-driven mechanisms in modified gravity to explain cosmic acceleration, or applying the 5-D space-time-temperature framework to other early-universe phase transitions.
idea_spark.risk: The theory is highly formal and lacks direct observational tests; the physical interpretation of the fifth dimension may be contested, and detailed numerical fitting with existing cosmological models is pending.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires reconstructing gravitational field equations from thermodynamic or informational principles, or exploring models linking quantum entanglement and spacetime emergence in the early universe.

papers.title: Geometric Metrics for MoE Specialization: From Fisher Information to Early Failure Detection
papers.summary: Expert specialization is fundamental to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model success, yet existing metrics (cosine similarity, routing entropy) lack theoretical grounding and yield inconsistent conclusions under reparameterization. We present an information-geometric framework providing the first rigorous characterization of MoE specialization dynamics. Our key insight is that expert routing distributions evolve on the probability simplex equipped with the Fisher information metric, enabling formal analysis via Riemannian geometry. We prove that standard heuristic metrics violate parameterization invariance (Theorem 1), establish that specialization corresponds to geodesic flow with quantified approximation bounds (Theorem 2), and derive a failure predictor with theoretical threshold justification (Theorem 3). The framework introduces two principled metrics: Fisher Specialization Index (FSI) achieving r=0.91+/-0.02 correlation with downstream performance, and Fisher Heterogeneity Score (FHS) predicting training failure at 10% completion with AUC=0.89+/-0.03 -- outperforming validation-loss-based early stopping by 23% while requiring 40x fewer compute cycles. We validate intervention protocols achieving 87% recovery rate when FHS>1 is detected. Comprehensive experiments across language modeling (WikiText-103, C4), vision MoE (ImageNet), and scaling studies (8-64 experts, 125M-2.7B parameters) validate our theoretical predictions.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一个基于信息几何的框架，首次为MoE模型的专家专业化动态提供了严格的理论刻画，并引入了两个新的度量指标FSI和FHS，用于量化专业化和预测训练失败。
ai.motivation: 现有的MoE专家专业化度量指标（如余弦相似度、路由熵）缺乏理论基础，且在参数化变换下结论不一致，需要一种具有理论保证的、不变的度量方法。
ai.method: 将专家路由分布视为概率单纯形上的点，并引入Fisher信息度量，利用黎曼几何进行形式化分析。提出了Fisher专业化指数（FSI）和Fisher异质性分数（FHS）两个原则性度量。
ai.result: FSI与下游性能的相关性达到0.91±0.02。FHS能在训练完成10%时预测失败，AUC为0.89±0.03，比基于验证损失的早停法性能提升23%，且计算量减少40倍。当检测到FHS>1时，干预协议可实现87%的恢复率。
ai.help_to_user: 为MoE模型的训练监控和诊断提供了理论可靠的工具，帮助研究者更早、更准确地识别训练问题并采取干预措施，节省计算资源。

idea_spark.idea: 利用概率分布空间的几何结构（如Fisher度量）来定义模型组件行为的固有、不变的度量，从而避免参数化依赖的伪影。
idea_spark.risk: 理论框架的假设（如路由分布演化在特定几何结构上）可能不适用于所有MoE变体或训练场景。新指标的计算可能引入额外开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考其他神经网络内部状态（如注意力分布、激活模式）是否也可以放在合适的几何空间中进行更本质的分析和度量。

en.tldr: This paper presents an information-geometric framework providing the first rigorous characterization of MoE specialization dynamics, introducing two new metrics, FSI and FHS, for quantifying specialization and predicting training failure.
en.motivation: Existing metrics for MoE expert specialization (e.g., cosine similarity, routing entropy) lack theoretical grounding and yield inconsistent conclusions under reparameterization, necessitating invariant metrics with theoretical guarantees.
en.method: Treats expert routing distributions as points on the probability simplex equipped with the Fisher information metric, enabling formal analysis via Riemannian geometry. Introduces two principled metrics: Fisher Specialization Index (FSI) and Fisher Heterogeneity Score (FHS).
en.result: FSI achieves a correlation of 0.91±0.02 with downstream performance. FHS predicts training failure at 10% completion with AUC=0.89±0.03, outperforming validation-loss-based early stopping by 23% while requiring 40x fewer compute cycles. Intervention protocols achieve an 87% recovery rate when FHS>1 is detected.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretically sound tools for monitoring and diagnosing MoE model training, helping researchers identify issues earlier and more accurately, and take corrective actions, thereby saving computational resources.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize the geometric structure of probability distribution spaces (e.g., the Fisher metric) to define intrinsic, invariant measures of component behavior, avoiding parameterization-dependent artifacts.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical assumptions (e.g., routing distribution evolution on a specific geometric structure) may not hold for all MoE variants or training scenarios. Computing the new metrics may introduce overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of whether other internal neural network states (e.g., attention distributions, activation patterns) can also be analyzed and measured more fundamentally within appropriate geometric spaces.

papers.title: A Comparative Study of CNN Optimization Methods for Edge AI: Exploring the Role of Early Exits
papers.summary: Deploying deep neural networks on edge devices requires balancing accuracy, latency, and resource constraints under realistic execution conditions. To fit models within these constraints, two broad strategies have emerged: static compression techniques such as pruning and quantization, which permanently reduce model size, and dynamic approaches such as early-exit mechanisms, which adapt computational cost at runtime. While both families are widely studied in isolation, they are rarely compared under identical conditions on physical hardware. This paper presents a unified deployment-oriented comparison of static compression and dynamic early-exit mechanisms, evaluated on real edge devices using ONNX based inference pipelines. Our results show that static and dynamic techniques offer fundamentally different trade-offs for edge deployment. While pruning and quantization deliver consistent memory footprint reduction, early-exit mechanisms enable input-adaptive computation savings that static methods cannot match. Their combination proves highly effective, simultaneously reducing inference latency and memory usage with minimal accuracy loss, expanding what is achievable at the edge.

ai.tldr: 本文在真实边缘设备上，通过ONNX推理流水线，统一比较了静态压缩（剪枝、量化）与动态早退机制在CNN优化中的表现。结果表明，两者提供根本不同的权衡，而结合使用可同时降低延迟和内存占用，且精度损失最小。
ai.motivation: 在边缘设备上部署深度神经网络需要在精度、延迟和资源约束之间取得平衡。静态压缩和动态早退是两种主要策略，但很少在相同硬件条件下进行直接比较。
ai.method: 在真实边缘设备上使用ONNX推理流水线，对静态压缩（剪枝、量化）和动态早退机制进行统一的部署导向比较。
ai.result: 静态压缩能持续减少内存占用，而早退机制能实现静态方法无法达到的输入自适应计算节省。两者结合可同时降低推理延迟和内存使用，且精度损失最小。
ai.help_to_user: 为在边缘设备上部署CNN提供了实证比较，明确了静态压缩与动态早退的不同权衡，并证明结合策略的有效性。

idea_spark.idea: 将静态压缩的稳定资源节省与动态早退的输入自适应计算节省相结合，形成混合优化策略。
idea_spark.risk: 组合策略可能增加系统复杂性，且对不同网络架构和任务类型的泛化能力需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在边缘AI中探索更多静态与动态技术的协同组合，并建立标准化的硬件基准测试流程。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在边缘AI中探索更多静态与动态技术的协同组合，并建立标准化的硬件基准测试流程。

en.tldr: This paper presents a unified comparison of static compression (pruning, quantization) and dynamic early-exit mechanisms for CNN optimization on real edge devices using ONNX inference pipelines. Results show fundamentally different trade-offs, and their combination effectively reduces both latency and memory usage with minimal accuracy loss.
en.motivation: Deploying deep neural networks on edge devices requires balancing accuracy, latency, and resource constraints. Static compression and dynamic early-exit are two major strategies but are rarely compared under identical hardware conditions.
en.method: A unified deployment-oriented comparison of static compression and dynamic early-exit mechanisms evaluated on real edge devices using ONNX-based inference pipelines.
en.result: Static compression delivers consistent memory footprint reduction, while early-exit enables input-adaptive computation savings unattainable by static methods. Their combination simultaneously reduces inference latency and memory usage with minimal accuracy loss.
en.help_to_user: Provides an empirical comparison for CNN deployment on edge devices, clarifies the different trade-offs of static vs. dynamic approaches, and demonstrates the effectiveness of combined strategies.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the stable resource savings of static compression with the input-adaptive computation savings of early-exit mechanisms into a hybrid optimization strategy.
idea_spark.risk: Combined strategies may increase system complexity, and generalization across different network architectures and task types requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of more synergistic combinations of static and dynamic techniques in edge AI, and the establishment of standardized hardware benchmarking procedures.

papers.title: Constructions of $q$-ary Golay Complementary Pairs Over Flexible Non-Power-of-Two Lengths
papers.summary: Golay complementary pair (GCP), first introduced by Golay in 1951, has been extensively studied and widely applied in communication systems. A $q$-ary GCP $\{\mathbf{A},\mathbf{B}\}$ consists of two $q$-ary complex sequences $\mathbf{A}=(A_0,\cdots,A_{M-1})$ and $\mathbf{B}=({B}_0,\cdots,{B}_{M-1})$ of equal length $M$, where $\textit{A}_i,\textit{B}_i\in\{ξ^a:0\leq a\leq q-1\}$ with $ξ=e^{\frac{2π\sqrt{-1}}{q}}$.In this paper,we prove that the existence of a quaternary ($q=4$) GCP of length $M$ is equivalent to the explicit constructibility of ($4h$)-ary GCPs of length $2^mM$ for all integers $h,m\geq1$. All proposed sequences are constructed via extended Boolean functions (EBFs), and the direct construction yields GCPs with more flexible length ranges than all previous relevant results.

ai.tldr: 本文证明了四元Golay互补对的存在性等价于(4h)元GCP在特定长度下的可构造性，并通过扩展布尔函数实现了比以往更灵活的非2幂次长度的直接构造。
ai.motivation: Golay互补对在通信系统中具有重要应用，但现有构造方法在长度灵活性方面存在限制，特别是非2幂次长度的q元GCP构造问题尚未完全解决。
ai.method: 采用扩展布尔函数（EBFs）作为构造工具，通过数学证明将四元GCP的存在性问题转化为(4h)元GCP的系统构造框架。
ai.result: 建立了四元GCP存在性与(4h)元GCP可构造性的等价关系，并实现了比所有先前结果更灵活的长度范围的直接构造。
ai.help_to_user: 为通信系统设计提供了更灵活的序列选择方案，特别是在需要非标准长度序列的应用场景中。

idea_spark.idea: 利用代数等价关系将特定进制序列的存在性问题转化为更一般进制序列的系统构造方法
idea_spark.risk: 实际工程应用中可能需要考虑计算复杂性和硬件实现约束
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过数学结构的深入分析发现不同进制序列构造之间的内在联系，为序列设计提供新范式

en.tldr: This paper proves the equivalence between the existence of quaternary Golay complementary pairs and the explicit constructibility of (4h)-ary GCPs at specific lengths, achieving more flexible non-power-of-two length constructions via extended Boolean functions.
en.motivation: Golay complementary pairs have important applications in communication systems, but existing construction methods have limitations in length flexibility, particularly for q-ary GCPs of non-power-of-two lengths.
en.method: Using extended Boolean functions (EBFs) as construction tools, the paper mathematically transforms the existence problem of quaternary GCPs into a systematic construction framework for (4h)-ary GCPs.
en.result: Established the equivalence between quaternary GCP existence and (4h)-ary GCP constructibility, achieving direct constructions with more flexible length ranges than all previous results.
en.help_to_user: Provides more flexible sequence selection options for communication system design, particularly in applications requiring non-standard length sequences.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize algebraic equivalence to transform existence problems of specific-ary sequences into systematic construction methods for more general-ary sequences
idea_spark.risk: Practical engineering applications may need to consider computational complexity and hardware implementation constraints
idea_spark.inspiration: Discovering intrinsic connections between different-ary sequence constructions through deep mathematical structure analysis provides a new paradigm for sequence design

papers.title: ControlFoley: Unified and Controllable Video-to-Audio Generation with Cross-Modal Conflict Handling
papers.summary: Recent advances in video-to-audio (V2A) generation enable high-quality audio synthesis from visual content, yet achieving robust and fine-grained controllability remains challenging. Existing methods suffer from weak textual controllability under visual-text conflict and imprecise stylistic control due to entangled temporal and timbre information in reference audio. Moreover, the lack of standardized benchmarks limits systematic evaluation.   We propose ControlFoley, a unified multimodal V2A framework that enables precise control over video, text, and reference audio. We introduce a joint visual encoding paradigm that integrates CLIP with a spatio-temporal audio-visual encoder to improve alignment and textual controllability. We further propose temporal-timbre decoupling to suppress redundant temporal cues while preserving discriminative timbre features. In addition, we design a modality-robust training scheme with unified multimodal representation alignment (REPA) and random modality dropout. We also present VGGSound-TVC, a benchmark for evaluating textual controllability under varying degrees of visual-text conflict.   Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across multiple V2A tasks, including text-guided, text-controlled, and audio-controlled generation. ControlFoley achieves superior controllability under cross-modal conflict while maintaining strong synchronization and audio quality, and shows competitive or better performance compared to an industrial V2A system.   Code, models, datasets, and demos are available at: https://yjx-research.github.io/ControlFoley/.

ai.tldr: ControlFoley是一个统一的视频到音频生成框架，通过跨模态冲突处理和时序-音色解耦实现精确的多模态控制。
ai.motivation: 现有视频到音频生成方法在视觉-文本冲突下文本控制性弱，且参考音频中时序与音色信息纠缠导致风格控制不精确，缺乏标准化评估基准。
ai.method: 提出联合视觉编码范式整合CLIP与时空视听编码器；使用时序-音色解耦抑制冗余时序线索；设计包含统一多模态表示对齐和随机模态丢弃的模态鲁棒训练方案；构建VGGSound-TVC基准数据集。
ai.result: 在文本引导、文本控制和音频控制生成等多个任务上达到先进性能，在跨模态冲突下实现优越可控性，同时保持强同步性和音频质量。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要从视频生成高质量、可精确控制音频的研究者提供统一框架和评估基准，支持视频、文本和参考音频的多模态控制。

idea_spark.idea: 在训练中引入随机模态丢弃增强模型对不完整输入的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 解耦的彻底性可能影响生成音频的自然度和连贯性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 联合编码范式为多模态对齐提供了新思路，可用于改进其他视听任务。

en.tldr: ControlFoley is a unified video-to-audio generation framework enabling precise multimodal control through cross-modal conflict handling and temporal-timbre decoupling.
en.motivation: Existing V2A methods suffer from weak textual controllability under visual-text conflict, imprecise stylistic control due to entangled temporal-timbre information, and lack of standardized benchmarks.
en.method: Proposes joint visual encoding integrating CLIP with spatio-temporal audio-visual encoder; employs temporal-timbre decoupling to suppress redundant temporal cues; designs modality-robust training with unified multimodal representation alignment and random modality dropout; introduces VGGSound-TVC benchmark.

en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework and benchmark for researchers needing high-quality, precisely controllable audio generation from video, supporting multimodal control via video, text, and reference audio.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing random modality dropout during training enhances model robustness to incomplete inputs.
idea_spark.risk: Overly aggressive decoupling may compromise naturalness and coherence of generated audio.
idea_spark.inspiration: The joint encoding paradigm offers new insights for multimodal alignment applicable to other audio-visual tasks.

papers.title: CoDaS: AI Co-Data-Scientist for Biomarker Discovery via Wearable Sensors
papers.summary: Scientific discovery in digital health requires converting continuous physiological signals from wearable devices into clinically actionable biomarkers. We introduce CoDaS (AI Co-Data-Scientist), a multi-agent system that structures biomarker discovery as an iterative process combining hypothesis generation, statistical analysis, adversarial validation, and literature-grounded reasoning with human oversight using large-scale wearable datasets. Across three cohorts totaling 9,279 participant-observations, CoDaS identified 41 candidate digital biomarkers for mental health and 25 for metabolic outcomes, each subjected to an internal validation battery spanning replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power. Across two independent depression cohorts, CoDaS surfaced circadian instability-related features in both datasets, reflected in sleep duration variability (DWB, ρ= 0.252, p < 0.001) and sleep onset variability (GLOBEM, ρ= 0.126, p < 0.001). In a metabolic cohort, CoDaS derived a cardiovascular fitness index (steps/resting heart rate; ρ= -0.374, p < 0.001), and recovered established clinical associations, including the hepatic function ratio (AST/ALT; ρ= -0.375, p < 0.001), a known correlate of insulin resistance. Incorporating CoDaS-derived features alongside demographic variables led to modest but consistent improvements in predictive performance, with cross-validated ΔR^2 increases of 0.040 for depression and 0.021 for insulin resistance. These findings suggest that CoDaS enables systematic and traceable hypothesis generation and prioritization for biomarker discovery from large-scale wearable data.

ai.tldr: CoDaS是一个多智能体AI系统，用于从大规模可穿戴设备数据中系统性地发现数字生物标志物，在心理健康和代谢健康领域识别出多个候选标志物并进行了验证。
ai.motivation: 数字健康领域的科学发现需要将可穿戴设备的连续生理信号转化为临床可操作的生物标志物，但这一过程复杂且需要系统化方法。
ai.method: 构建了一个多智能体系统（CoDaS），将生物标志物发现构建为一个结合假设生成、统计分析、对抗性验证和文献推理的迭代过程，并辅以人工监督。在总计9279名参与者的三个队列中应用。
ai.result: CoDaS识别出41个心理健康候选数字生物标志物和25个代谢健康候选标志物。在独立抑郁队列中发现了与昼夜节律不稳定性相关的特征（如睡眠持续时间变异性）。在代谢队列中推导出心血管健康指数并恢复了已知的临床关联（如AST/ALT比率）。加入CoDaS特征后，抑郁和胰岛素抵抗的预测性能有适度但一致的提升（ΔR²分别为0.040和0.021）。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个用于从可穿戴数据中系统化发现和验证数字生物标志物的AI框架，支持假设生成、优先级排序和可追溯的分析。

idea_spark.idea: 将生物标志物发现构建为多智能体协作的、人机交互的、可追溯的迭代科学过程。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于大规模高质量数据集，发现的生物标志物需要进一步的临床验证和解释。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他复杂科学发现任务（如药物发现、材料科学）提供了一种结构化的、AI辅助的假设生成与验证范式。

en.tldr: CoDaS is a multi-agent AI system for systematic digital biomarker discovery from large-scale wearable data, identifying and validating candidate biomarkers for mental and metabolic health.
en.motivation: Scientific discovery in digital health requires converting continuous physiological signals from wearables into clinically actionable biomarkers, a process that demands systematic and scalable approaches.

idea_spark.idea: Structure biomarker discovery as a multi-agent, human-in-the-loop, traceable, iterative scientific process.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on large-scale, high-quality datasets; discovered biomarkers require further clinical validation and interpretation.

papers.title: EdgeDetect: Importance-Aware Gradient Compression with Homomorphic Aggregation for Federated Intrusion Detection
papers.summary: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative intrusion detection without raw data exchange, but conventional FL incurs high communication overhead from full-precision gradient transmission and remains vulnerable to gradient inference attacks. This paper presents EdgeDetect, a communication-efficient and privacy-aware federated IDS for bandwidth-constrained 6G-IoT environments. EdgeDetect introduces gradient smartification, a median-based statistical binarization that compresses local updates to $\{+1,-1\}$ representations, reducing uplink payload by $32\times$ while preserving convergence. We further integrate Paillier homomorphic encryption over binarized gradients, protecting against honest-but-curious servers without exposing individual updates. Experiments on CIC-IDS2017 (2.8M flows, 7 attack classes) demonstrate $98.0\%$ multi-class accuracy and $97.9\%$ macro F1-score, matching centralized baselines, while reducing per-round communication from $450$~MB to $14$~MB ($96.9\%$ reduction). Raspberry Pi-4 deployment confirms edge feasibility: $4.2$~MB memory, $0.8$~ms latency, and $12$~mJ per inference with $<0.5\%$ accuracy loss. Under $5\%$ poisoning attacks and severe imbalance, EdgeDetect maintains $87\%$ accuracy and $0.95$ minority class F1 ($p<0.001$), establishing a practical accuracy, communication, and privacy tradeoff for next-generation edge intrusion detection.

ai.tldr: EdgeDetect：一种用于联邦入侵检测的通信高效且隐私感知的框架，通过梯度智能化和同态聚合实现。
ai.motivation: 传统联邦学习在入侵检测中存在通信开销高和梯度推理攻击风险的问题，尤其在带宽受限的6G-IoT环境中。
ai.method: 提出梯度智能化（基于中值的统计二值化）将本地更新压缩为{+1,-1}表示，并结合Paillier同态加密保护梯度隐私。
ai.result: 在CIC-IDS2017数据集上达到98.0%多类准确率和97.9%宏F1分数，每轮通信从450MB减少至14MB（减少96.9%），在树莓派4上实现低资源消耗。
ai.help_to_user: 为资源受限的边缘环境提供了一种高精度、低通信、强隐私的入侵检测解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将统计驱动的二值化与加密技术结合，在压缩通信量的同时防止服务器窥探。
idea_spark.risk: 二值化可能在高维或复杂模型中损失信息；同态加密计算开销需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在边缘设备上部署轻量级隐私保护联邦学习模型，平衡效率与安全。

en.tldr: EdgeDetect: A communication-efficient and privacy-aware federated intrusion detection framework using importance-aware gradient compression with homomorphic aggregation.
en.motivation: Conventional federated learning for intrusion detection suffers from high communication overhead and vulnerability to gradient inference attacks, especially in bandwidth-constrained 6G-IoT environments.
en.method: Proposes gradient smartification (median-based statistical binarization) to compress local updates to {+1,-1} representations, integrated with Paillier homomorphic encryption for privacy protection.
en.result: Achieves 98.0% multi-class accuracy and 97.9% macro F1-score on CIC-IDS2017, reducing per-round communication from 450MB to 14MB (96.9% reduction), with low resource consumption on Raspberry Pi 4.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-accuracy, low-communication, and strong-privacy intrusion detection solution for resource-constrained edge environments.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate statistically-driven binarization with encryption to compress communication while preventing server curiosity.
idea_spark.risk: Binarization may lose information in high-dimensional or complex models; homomorphic encryption computational overhead needs further optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires deployment of lightweight privacy-preserving federated learning models on edge devices, balancing efficiency and security.

papers.title: Agentic Microphysics: A Manifesto for Generative AI Safety
papers.summary: This paper advances a methodological proposal for safety research in agentic AI. As systems acquire planning, memory, tool use, persistent identity, and sustained interaction, safety can no longer be analysed primarily at the level of the isolated model. Population-level risks arise from structured interaction among agents, through processes of communication, observation, and mutual influence that shape collective behaviour over time. As the object of analysis shifts, a methodological gap emerges. Approaches focused either on single agents or on aggregate outcomes do not identify the interaction-level mechanisms that generate collective risks or the design variables that control them. A framework is required that links local interaction structure to population-level dynamics in a causally explicit way, allowing both explanation and intervention. We introduce two linked concepts. Agentic microphysics defines the level of analysis: local interaction dynamics where one agent's output becomes another's input under specific protocol conditions. Generative safety defines the methodology: growing phenomena and elicit risks from micro-level conditions to identify sufficient mechanisms, detect thresholds, and design effective interventions.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种用于智能体AI安全研究的方法论框架，主张从孤立模型分析转向关注智能体间结构化交互产生的群体风险。
ai.motivation: 随着AI系统获得规划、记忆、工具使用等能力，安全分析不能再局限于单个模型，需关注智能体间交互产生的群体级风险。
ai.method: 引入两个核心概念：智能体微观物理学（分析局部交互动态）和生成式安全（从微观条件推导宏观现象与风险的方法论）。
ai.result: 建立连接局部交互结构与群体动态的因果显式框架，支持风险解释与干预设计。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究多智能体系统中涌现性风险提供分析层级与方法论工具。

idea_spark.idea: 设计可调节交互协议的实验平台，观察不同协议下群体行为的相变阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 过度简化交互模型可能遗漏关键风险传导路径。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受统计物理学与社会动力学启发，将群体行为视为微观交互的涌现现象。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a methodological framework for agentic AI safety research, shifting analysis from isolated models to population-level risks arising from structured agent interactions.
en.motivation: As AI systems acquire planning, memory, and tool-use capabilities, safety analysis must extend beyond single models to address collective risks emerging from agent interactions.
en.method: Introduces two linked concepts: Agentic Microphysics (analyzing local interaction dynamics) and Generative Safety (methodology for deriving phenomena and risks from micro-level conditions).
en.result: Establishes a causally explicit framework linking local interaction structure to population-level dynamics, enabling both explanation and intervention.
en.help_to_user: Provides analytical levels and methodological tools for studying emergent risks in multi-agent systems.

idea_spark.idea: Design experimental platforms with adjustable interaction protocols to observe phase transition thresholds in collective behavior.
idea_spark.risk: Over-simplified interaction models may miss critical risk transmission pathways.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by statistical physics and social dynamics, treating collective behavior as emergent phenomena from microscopic interactions.

papers.title: Complexity of an inexact stochastic SQP algorithm for equality constrained optimization
papers.summary: In this paper, we consider nonlinear optimization problems with a stochastic objective function and deterministic equality constraints. We propose an inexact two-stepsize stochastic sequential quadratic programming (SQP) algorithm and analyze its worst-case complexity under mild assumptions. The method utilizes a step decomposition strategy and handles stochastic gradient estimates by assigning different stepsizes to different components of the search direction. We establish the first known $\mathcal{O}(ε_c^{-2})$ worst-case complexity with respect to the infeasibility measure when no constraint qualification is assumed and a worst-case complexity of $\mathcal{O}(ε_c^{-1})$ when LICQ holds, matching the best known result in the literature. In addition, under mild conditions, our method achieves the optimal $\mathcal{O}(ε_L^{-4})$ complexity with respect to the gradient of the Lagrangian regardless of constraint qualifications. Our results provide the first complexity guarantees for the popular Byrd-Omojukun step decomposition strategy and verify its theoretical efficacy. Numerical experiments show that our algorithm has a superior infeasibility convergence performance and a competitive KKT convergence rate compared to the state-of-the-art stochastic SQP method.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于等式约束随机优化的不精确双步长随机SQP算法，并分析了其最坏情况复杂度。
ai.motivation: 处理随机目标函数与确定性等式约束的非线性优化问题，需要高效的算法与可靠的理论保证。
ai.method: 采用步长分解策略，对搜索方向的不同分量分配不同的步长来处理随机梯度估计，形成两阶段随机SQP算法。
ai.result: 在无约束品性假设下，获得了关于不可行性度量的O(ε_c^{-2})最坏情况复杂度；在LICQ成立时，复杂度为O(ε_c^{-1})；关于拉格朗日梯度，达到了最优的O(ε_L^{-4})复杂度。数值实验表明算法在不可行性收敛和KKT收敛率上具有竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理随机等式约束优化问题提供了一个具有严格复杂度保证的算法框架，并首次为Byrd-Omojukun步分解策略提供了理论验证。

idea_spark.idea: 将搜索方向分解为不同分量并分配差异化步长，以平衡可行性下降与目标函数下降。
idea_spark.risk: 算法性能依赖于随机梯度的估计质量，在噪声较大时可能需要调整步长策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可扩展至处理不等式约束或更一般的约束品性条件。

en.tldr: This paper proposes an inexact two-stepsize stochastic SQP algorithm for equality constrained stochastic optimization and analyzes its worst-case complexity.
en.motivation: To address nonlinear optimization problems with stochastic objective functions and deterministic equality constraints, requiring efficient algorithms with reliable theoretical guarantees.
en.method: Employs a step decomposition strategy, assigning different stepsizes to different components of the search direction to handle stochastic gradient estimates, forming a two-phase stochastic SQP algorithm.
en.result: Establishes an O(ε_c^{-2}) worst-case complexity for the infeasibility measure without constraint qualification assumptions, and O(ε_c^{-1}) when LICQ holds. Achieves the optimal O(ε_L^{-4}) complexity for the gradient of the Lagrangian. Numerical experiments show competitive infeasibility convergence and KKT convergence rates.
en.help_to_user: Provides an algorithmic framework with rigorous complexity guarantees for stochastic equality constrained optimization, and offers the first theoretical verification for the Byrd-Omojukun step decomposition strategy.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose the search direction into components with differentiated stepsizes to balance feasibility reduction and objective function descent.
idea_spark.risk: Algorithm performance depends on the quality of stochastic gradient estimates; may require step-size adjustment under high noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: The framework could be extended to handle inequality constraints or more general constraint qualification conditions.

papers.title: Reed--Muller Codes Achieve the Symmetric Capacity on Finite-State Channels
papers.summary: We study reliable communication over finite-state channels (FSCs) using Reed--Muller (RM) codes. Building on recent symmetry-based analyses for memoryless channels, we show that a sequence of binary RM codes (with some random scrambling) can achieve the symmetric capacity (or uniform-input information rate) of a binary-input indecomposable FSC.   Our approach has three components. First, we establish a capacity-via-symmetry theorem for doubly-transitive group codes on discrete memoryless channels (DMCs) with non-binary inputs, under some symmetry and puncturing conditions. Then, we reduce a binary-input FSC to an almost memoryless non-binary channel by grouping adjacent input bits into blocks and interleaving non-binary codes onto the channel. Finally, we show that the interleaved non-binary codes can be constructed from a single binary RM code.

ai.tldr: 本文证明了经过随机扰乱的二进制 Reed-Muller 码序列可以在二进制输入不可分解有限状态信道上达到对称容量。
ai.motivation: 研究在有限状态信道上实现可靠通信，扩展对称性分析从无记忆信道到有记忆信道。
ai.method: 首先为离散无记忆信道建立基于对称性的容量定理；然后将二进制输入有限状态信道通过分组和交织转化为近似无记忆的非二进制信道；最后证明交织的非二进制码可由单个二进制 RM 码构造。
ai.result: 二进制 Reed-Muller 码（经随机扰乱）能够达到二进制输入不可分解有限状态信道的对称容量（或均匀输入信息率）。
ai.help_to_user: 为有限状态信道提供了一种具体的、可达到容量的编码方案，扩展了 RM 码的应用范围。

idea_spark.idea: 使用 RM 码的代数对称性来逼近有限状态信道的容量，通过交织将二进制码映射到非二进制信道。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于信道的不可分解性和对称性条件；随机扰乱的实际实现可能增加复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 对称性在编码理论中的核心作用；如何利用码的结构特性来克服信道记忆效应。

en.tldr: This paper proves that sequences of binary Reed-Muller codes (with random scrambling) can achieve the symmetric capacity on binary-input indecomposable finite-state channels.
en.motivation: To study reliable communication over finite-state channels, extending symmetry-based analyses from memoryless to channels with memory.
en.method: First establish a capacity-via-symmetry theorem for doubly-transitive group codes on discrete memoryless channels; then reduce a binary-input FSC to an almost memoryless non-binary channel by grouping and interleaving; finally show the interleaved non-binary codes can be constructed from a single binary RM code.
en.result: Binary Reed-Muller codes (with random scrambling) achieve the symmetric capacity (or uniform-input information rate) of binary-input indecomposable finite-state channels.
en.help_to_user: Provides a concrete, capacity-achieving coding scheme for finite-state channels, extending the applicability of RM codes.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize the algebraic symmetry of RM codes to approach the capacity of finite-state channels, mapping binary codes to non-binary channels via interleaving.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on channel indecomposability and symmetry conditions; practical implementation of random scrambling may increase complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: The central role of symmetry in coding theory; how to leverage code structure properties to overcome channel memory effects.

papers.title: Multigrain-aware Semantic Prototype Scanning and Tri-Token Prompt Learning Embraced High-Order RWKV for Pan-Sharpening
papers.summary: In this work, we propose a Multigrain-aware Semantic Prototype Scanning paradigm for pan-sharpening, built upon a high-order RWKV architecture and a tri-token prompting mechanism derived from semantic clustering. Specifically, our method contains three key components: 1) Multigrain-aware Semantic Prototype Scanning. Although RWKV offers a efficient linear-complexity alternative to Transformers, its conventional bidirectional raster scanning is still semantic-agnostic and prone to positional bias. To address this issue, we introduce a semantic-driven scanning strategy that leverages locality-sensitive hashing to group semantically related regions and construct multi-grain semantic prototypes, enabling context-aware token reordering and more coherent global interaction. 2) Tri-token Prompt Learning. We design a tri-token prompting mechanism consisting of a global token, cluster-derived prototype tokens, and a learnable register token. The global and prototype tokens provide complementary semantic priors for RWKV modeling, while the register token helps suppress noisy and artifact-prone intermediate representations. 3) Invertible Q-Shift. To counteract spatial details, we apply center difference convolution on the value pathway to inject high-frequency information, and introduce an invertible multi-scale Q-shift operation for efficient and lossless feature transformation without parameter-heavy receptive field expansion. Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our method.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于高阶RWKV架构的多粒度语义原型扫描与三令牌提示学习框架，用于全色锐化任务，通过语义驱动的扫描策略和可逆Q-Shift操作提升图像融合质量。
ai.motivation: 传统RWKV的双向光栅扫描缺乏语义感知且易受位置偏差影响，全色锐化任务需要更有效的全局交互和细节保持机制。
ai.method: 1) 多粒度语义原型扫描：利用局部敏感哈希对语义相关区域分组，构建多粒度语义原型以实现上下文感知的令牌重排。2) 三令牌提示学习：设计全局令牌、聚类原型令牌和可学习寄存器令牌的提示机制。3) 可逆Q-Shift：在值路径应用中心差分卷积注入高频信息，通过可逆多尺度Q-Shift实现无损特征变换。
ai.result: 实验结果表明该方法在全色锐化任务上具有优越性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为全色锐化任务提供了语义感知的RWKV改进方案，通过多粒度语义原型和提示机制增强模型对遥感图像的理解能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部敏感哈希与RWKV结合实现语义感知扫描，用可逆操作保持空间细节。
idea_spark.risk: 语义聚类对计算资源敏感，多粒度原型设计可能增加模型复杂度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将语义分组思想应用于其他线性复杂度架构，可逆操作可为轻量化模型设计提供参考。

en.tldr: Proposes a multigrain-aware semantic prototype scanning and tri-token prompt learning framework based on high-order RWKV for pan-sharpening, enhancing image fusion quality through semantic-driven scanning and invertible Q-Shift operations.
en.motivation: Conventional RWKV's bidirectional raster scanning lacks semantic awareness and suffers from positional bias, while pan-sharpening requires more effective global interaction and detail preservation mechanisms.
en.method: 1) Multigrain-aware Semantic Prototype Scanning: Uses locality-sensitive hashing to group semantically related regions and construct multi-grain semantic prototypes for context-aware token reordering. 2) Tri-token Prompt Learning: Designs a prompting mechanism with global tokens, cluster-derived prototype tokens, and learnable register tokens. 3) Invertible Q-Shift: Applies center difference convolution on the value pathway to inject high-frequency information and introduces invertible multi-scale Q-shift for lossless feature transformation.
en.result: Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method in pan-sharpening tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a semantic-aware RWKV improvement scheme for pan-sharpening, enhancing model understanding of remote sensing images through multigrain semantic prototypes and prompting mechanisms.

idea_spark.idea: Combine locality-sensitive hashing with RWKV for semantic-aware scanning, using invertible operations to preserve spatial details.
idea_spark.risk: Semantic clustering is computationally sensitive; multigrain prototype design may increase model complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying semantic grouping to other linear-complexity architectures; invertible operations provide references for lightweight model design.

papers.title: Disentangled Dual-Branch Graph Learning for Conversational Emotion Recognition
papers.summary: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations aims to infer utterance-level emotions by jointly modeling textual, acoustic, and visual cues within context. Despite recent progress, key challenges remain, including redundant cross-modal information, imperfect semantic alignment, and insufficient modeling of high-order speaker interactions. To address these issues, we propose a framework that combines dual-space feature disentanglement with dual-branch graph learning. A shared encoder and modality-specific encoders are used to separate modality-invariant and modality-specific representations. The invariant features are modeled by a Fourier graph neural network to capture global consistency and complementary patterns, with a frequency-domain contrastive objective to enhance discriminability. In parallel, a speaker-aware hypergraph is constructed over modality-specific features to model high-order interactions, along with a speaker-consistency constraint to maintain coherent semantics. Finally, the two branches are fused for utterance-level emotion prediction. Experiments on IEMOCAP and MELD demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance over strong baselines, validating its effectiveness.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合双空间特征解耦与双分支图学习的框架，用于对话中的多模态情感识别，通过分离模态不变/特定特征并分别建模全局一致性与高阶说话人交互来提升性能。
ai.motivation: 现有对话多模态情感识别方法面临跨模态信息冗余、语义对齐不完美、高阶说话人交互建模不足等挑战。
ai.method: 使用共享编码器和模态特定编码器分离模态不变与特定特征；不变特征通过傅里叶图神经网络建模全局一致性，并采用频域对比目标增强区分性；特定特征构建说话人感知超图以建模高阶交互，并施加说话人一致性约束；最后融合双分支进行情感预测。
ai.result: 在IEMOCAP和MELD数据集上超越现有强基线，验证了方法的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种通过特征解耦与图学习联合优化多模态对话情感识别的系统框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将频域对比学习与超图建模结合，分别处理全局模式与局部高阶交互。
idea_spark.risk: 对模态对齐质量与图结构构建的假设可能影响泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将频域分析与拓扑建模结合，以同时捕获一致性与互补性模式。

zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种通过特征解耦与图学习联合优化多模态对话情感识别的系统框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将频域对比学习与超图建模结合，分别处理全局模式与局部高阶交互。
idea_spark.risk: 对模态对齐质量与图结构构建的假设可能影响泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将频域分析与拓扑建模结合，以同时捕获一致性与互补性模式。

en.tldr: Proposes a framework combining dual-space feature disentanglement with dual-branch graph learning for multimodal emotion recognition in conversations, improving performance by separating modality-invariant/specific features and modeling global consistency and high-order speaker interactions separately.
en.motivation: Existing methods for multimodal emotion recognition in conversations face challenges including redundant cross-modal information, imperfect semantic alignment, and insufficient modeling of high-order speaker interactions.
en.method: Uses a shared encoder and modality-specific encoders to separate modality-invariant and modality-specific representations. Invariant features are modeled by a Fourier graph neural network for global consistency with a frequency-domain contrastive objective; specific features construct a speaker-aware hypergraph for high-order interactions with a speaker-consistency constraint. The two branches are fused for emotion prediction.
en.result: Outperforms strong baselines on IEMOCAP and MELD datasets, validating effectiveness.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic framework for jointly optimizing multimodal conversational emotion recognition through feature disentanglement and graph learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combining frequency-domain contrastive learning with hypergraph modeling to handle global patterns and local high-order interactions separately.
idea_spark.risk: Assumptions about modality alignment quality and graph construction may affect generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining frequency-domain analysis with topological modeling to capture both consistency and complementary patterns.

papers.title: DiscoTrace: Representing and Comparing Answering Strategies of Humans and LLMs in Information-Seeking Question Answering
papers.summary: We introduce DiscoTrace, a method to identify the rhetorical strategies that answerers use when responding to information-seeking questions. DiscoTrace represents answers as a sequence of question-related discourse acts paired with interpretations of the original question, annotated on top of rhetorical structure theory parses. Applying DiscoTrace to answers from nine different human communities reveals that communities have diverse preferences for answer construction. In contrast, LLMs do not exhibit rhetorical diversity in their answers, even when prompted to mimic specific human community answering guidelines. LLMs also systematically opt for breadth, addressing interpretations of questions that human answerers choose not to address. Our findings can guide the development of pragmatic LLM answerers that consider a range of strategies informed by context in QA.

ai.tldr: DiscoTrace是一种通过话语行为序列分析人类与LLM在信息寻求问答中回答策略差异的方法。
ai.motivation: 理解不同人类社区与LLM在构建信息寻求答案时使用的修辞策略差异，以指导开发更符合语境的LLM回答者。
ai.method: 基于修辞结构理论解析，将答案表示为与问题相关的话语行为序列，并标注对原始问题的解释。应用于九个不同人类社区的答案进行分析比较。
ai.result: 人类社区在答案构建上表现出多样化的修辞偏好，而LLM即使被提示模仿特定社区指南，其回答也缺乏修辞多样性，且系统性地选择更广泛的解释范围。
ai.help_to_user: 为分析问答策略提供了可操作的方法论框架，揭示了LLM与人类在信息呈现方式上的根本差异。

idea_spark.idea: 开发能够动态检测并适配目标社区修辞偏好的LLM回答生成系统。
idea_spark.risk: 过度适配特定社区风格可能导致答案失去客观性或泛化能力下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将社区文化差异量化为可计算的话语模式，为跨文化人机交互设计提供新视角。

zh.motivation: 理解不同人类社区与LLM在构建信息寻求答案时使用的修辞策略差异，以指导开发更符合语境的LLM回答者。

zh.help_to_user: 为分析问答策略提供了可操作的方法论框架，揭示了LLM与人类在信息呈现方式上的根本差异。

idea_spark.idea: 开发能够动态检测并适配目标社区修辞偏好的LLM回答生成系统。
idea_spark.risk: 过度适配特定社区风格可能导致答案失去客观性或泛化能力下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将社区文化差异量化为可计算的话语模式，为跨文化人机交互设计提供新视角。

en.tldr: DiscoTrace is a method that analyzes answering strategy differences between humans and LLMs in information-seeking QA through sequences of discourse acts.
en.motivation: To understand differences in rhetorical strategies used by diverse human communities versus LLMs when constructing answers, guiding development of more context-aware LLM answerers.
en.method: Represents answers as sequences of question-related discourse acts with interpretations annotated on rhetorical structure theory parses. Applied to answers from nine human communities for comparative analysis.
en.result: Human communities exhibit diverse rhetorical preferences in answer construction, while LLMs show limited rhetorical diversity even when prompted to mimic community guidelines, and systematically opt for broader question interpretations.
en.help_to_user: Provides an actionable methodological framework for analyzing QA strategies, revealing fundamental differences in information presentation between LLMs and humans.

idea_spark.idea: Develop LLM answer generation systems capable of dynamically detecting and adapting to target community rhetorical preferences.
idea_spark.risk: Over-adaptation to specific community styles may compromise answer objectivity or generalization capability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Quantifying cultural differences as computable discourse patterns offers new perspectives for cross-cultural human-computer interaction design.

papers.title: Faithfulness Serum: Mitigating the Faithfulness Gap in Textual Explanations of LLM Decisions via Attribution Guidance
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong performance and have revolutionized NLP, but their lack of explainability keeps them treated as black boxes, limiting their use in domains that demand transparency and trust. A promising direction to address this issue is post-hoc text-based explanations, which aim to explain model decisions in natural language. Prior work has focused on generating convincing rationales that appear to be subjectively faithful, but it remains unclear whether these explanations are epistemically faithful, whether they reflect the internal evidence the model actually relied on for its decision. In this paper, we first assess the epistemic faithfulness of LLM-generated explanations via counterfactuals and show that they are often unfaithful. We then introduce a training-free method that enhances faithfulness by guiding explanation generation through attention-level interventions, informed by token-level heatmaps extracted via a faithful attribution method. This method significantly improves epistemic faithfulness across multiple models, benchmarks, and prompts.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种无需训练的方法，通过基于忠实归因的注意力干预，提升LLM文本解释的忠实性，弥合解释与模型内部证据之间的差距。
ai.motivation: LLM的文本解释常缺乏忠实性，即解释未能反映模型决策所依赖的内部证据，这限制了其在需要透明度和信任的领域中的应用。
ai.method: 首先通过反事实评估揭示解释的忠实性差距；然后引入一种无需训练的方法，利用忠实归因方法提取的token级热图，通过注意力层干预来引导解释生成。
ai.result: 该方法在多个模型、基准和提示下显著提升了解释的忠实性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种提升LLM解释忠实性的实用工具，有助于在需要高透明度的场景中建立对模型决策的信任。

idea_spark.idea: 将归因分析作为实时约束注入生成过程，而非仅作事后分析。
idea_spark.risk: 归因方法本身的忠实性假设可能不成立；干预可能影响生成流畅性或多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似干预应用于多模态解释生成或决策溯源任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将归因分析作为实时约束注入生成过程，而非仅作事后分析。

idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似干预应用于多模态解释生成或决策溯源任务。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a training-free method that enhances the faithfulness of LLM textual explanations via attention-level interventions guided by faithful attribution heatmaps, mitigating the gap between explanations and the model's internal evidence.
en.motivation: Textual explanations from LLMs often lack epistemic faithfulness, i.e., they do not reflect the internal evidence the model actually relied on, limiting their use in domains requiring transparency and trust.
en.method: First assesses explanation faithfulness via counterfactuals; then introduces a training-free method that guides explanation generation through attention interventions informed by token-level heatmaps extracted via a faithful attribution method.
en.result: The method significantly improves epistemic faithfulness across multiple models, benchmarks, and prompts.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical tool to enhance the faithfulness of LLM explanations, aiding in building trust for model decisions in high-stakes transparent applications.

idea_spark.idea: Inject attribution analysis as a real-time constraint into the generation process rather than using it only for post-hoc analysis.
idea_spark.risk: The faithfulness assumption of the underlying attribution method may not hold; interventions might affect generation fluency or diversity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore applying similar interventions to multimodal explanation generation or decision provenance tasks.

papers.title: Mix-CALADIN: A Distributed Algorithm for Consensus Mixed-Integer Optimization
papers.summary: This paper addresses distributed consensus optimization problems with mixed-integer variables, with a specific focus on Boolean variables. We introduce a novel distributed algorithm that extends the Consensus Augmented Lagrangian Alternating Direction Inexact Newton (CALADIN) framework by incorporating specialized techniques for handling Boolean variables without relying on local mixed-integer solvers. Under the mild assumption of Lipschitz continuity of the objective functions, we establish rigorous convergence guarantees for both convex and nonconvex mixed-integer programming problems. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves competitive performance compared to existing approaches while providing rigorous convergence guarantees.

ai.tldr: 本文提出Mix-CALADIN算法，一种处理混合整数（特别是布尔变量）分布式共识优化的新方法，无需本地混合整数求解器，并在凸与非凸问题上提供收敛保证。
ai.motivation: 分布式共识优化问题中混合整数变量的处理，尤其是布尔变量，现有方法常依赖本地混合整数求解器，缺乏统一的收敛理论保证。
ai.method: 扩展CALADIN框架，引入专门处理布尔变量的技术，避免使用本地混合整数求解器，在目标函数Lipschitz连续的温和假设下进行设计。
ai.result: 算法在凸与非凸混合整数规划问题上均建立了严格的收敛保证，数值实验显示其性能与现有方法相当。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理混合整数（特别是布尔变量）的分布式优化问题提供了一个理论保证严格且无需本地专用求解器的算法框架。

idea_spark.idea: 在交替方向乘子法中嵌入专门的离散变量处理模块，避免调用黑箱求解器，以保持理论分析的纯洁性。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度非凸或大规模整数变量的实际计算效率可能仍需进一步验证；Lipschitz连续性假设在部分应用中可能较强。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考如何将经典连续优化算法与离散结构处理技术结合，为混合整数分布式优化提供新的算法设计范式。
